definition.en: This connecting framework does some of the translating for me that a reassembled ego would do. It delivers information to Ruburt in a way that he can understand.
definition.zh: 这个连接性框架为我做了部分翻译工作，这些工作原本由重组后的自我来完成。它以一种鲁柏能够理解的方式向他传递信息。

definition.en: It is actually the intangible but actual framework formed by inner energy, about whose lines or, actually, reference points, the physical framework is constructed.
definition.zh: 它实际上是由内在能量形成的无形但真实的框架，物理框架正是沿着它的线条或参考点构建起来的。

definition.en: In such cases the tragedy itself is chosen as an emotional framework upon which the psyche plays. The framework is not thrust upon it, but indeed chosen precisely because of its own characteristics -- even those of despondency, perhaps.
definition.zh: 在这种情况下，悲剧本身被选为一种情感框架，心灵在其上演奏。这框架并非强加于心灵，而是因其自身的特性——甚至可能是沮丧的特性——而被精确选择的。

definition.en: Later I will explain how this psychological framework is created and maintained, for it is like a road that must be kept clear of debris.
definition.zh: 稍后我将解释这个心理框架是如何被创造和维持的，因为它就像一条必须保持畅通的道路。

definition.en: In many cases this translation is done by the intervening psychological framework, which is simply the psychological point closest to the meeting of Ruburts personality and my own.
definition.zh: 在许多情况下，这种翻译是由介入的心理框架完成的，这个框架就是最接近鲁柏人格与我人格交汇点的心理点。

explanation: 「架构」是赛斯用来描述不同层次现实运作方式的核心概念。存在两种主要架构：架构一是你日常感知的物质世界，遵循因果、线性时间和物理法则；架构二是更广阔的内在现实，是思想、情感、概率和创造力的源头。架构二并非被动或中立，它天然倾向于建设性发展，积极的思想更容易在其中显化为现实。两个架构持续互动：你在架构二中进行真正的创造性工作，然后通过内在自我将其转化为架构一中的具体事件。梦境、艺术和直觉都是架构二信息以象征形式进入架构一的翻译过程。每个存在——无论人类、物体还是其他现实中的自我——都拥有自己的架构组合。架构赋予事件“不可见的厚度”，使现实具有多维基础。科学和宗教等体系本身也是一种架构，

quotes.text: I speak as my whole self to you ... since my personality structure is more advanced than is usual for communications from other systems. Therefore, I do not need to adopt a past ego [of my own]. Perhaps because this is not necessary, the psychological bridge is required to make my messages comprehensible to Ruburt. This connecting framework does some of the translating for me that a reassembled ego would do. It delivers information to Ruburt in a way that he can understand. Occasionally [in your tests] I do impress him directly, telepathically (see Note 19), with a concept. When he receives data in the form of images the framework is operating. With my direction, this framework uses Ruburt's personal associations to direct his impressions toward the correct point. Then when we are successful I insert the right information.23
quotes.translation: 我以完整的自己对你说话……因为我的人格结构比来自其他系统的通讯更为先进。因此，我不需要采用[我自己的]过去自我。或许正因无此必要，才需要心理桥梁让我的讯息能被鲁柏理解。这个连接框架替我完成了重组自我本应做的部分翻译工作。它以鲁柏能理解的方式传递信息。偶尔[在你们的测试中]我会直接用意念感应（见注19）将概念印入他心中。当他以图像形式接收数据时，这个框架就在运作。在我的指引下，这个框架利用鲁柏的个人联想，引导他的印象指向正确方向。当我们成功时，我便植入正确信息。23

source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: But if the interactions between or among frameworks exist for everybody, in our terms, then as far as I'm concerned they exist for each thing as well--and I do mean the so-called "inanimate." (This isn't the place to go into it, but Seth maintains that for many reasons we arbitrarily decide what's living and nonliving.) Each reincarnational self, each counterpart self and probable self has its complement of frameworks. So does the most minute living or nonliving entity and the most gigantic. So, "probably," do most of the far-out probable realities one can imagine--for I won't go so far as to deny that some probable realities may exist without such framework structures. Strange one-dimensional "flatlands" indeed! But in each case where those framework interactions operate, they help each creation, each presence, each essence or vital principle fulfill "a larger picture that the conscious mind may not be able presently to perceive." In ways I can't even begin to describe here, all frameworks must ultimately be joined within the ineffable context of All That Is.
quotes.translation: 但若框架间的互动存在于每个人身上，按我们的说法，那么在我看来它们也存在于每个事物中——我指的是所谓的"无生命物"。（此处不宜深入探讨，但赛斯坚持认为我们基于诸多理由武断地划分了生命与非生命。）每个转世自我、每个对应自我和可能自我都有其框架的补充。最微小的生命或非生命实体如此，最庞大的实体亦然。因此，"很可能"连人们能想象的大多数遥远可能实相也是如此——因为我不会武断否认某些可能实相可能不存在此类框架结构。那真是奇异的一维"扁平世界"！但在每个框架互动运作的案例中，它们都帮助每个创造、每个存在、每个本质或生命原则实现"意识心目前可能无法感知的更大图景"。以我在此无法描述的方式，所有框架最终都必须在万有不可思议的语境中融为一体。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: I don't really think we can conceive of anything to be truly "alien," though, so I use the word here only to lead into the next of Seth's larger concepts that I want to mention: that of probable realities, or probabilities, as Jane and I usually say. Not only does Seth stress the constant psychic motion of reincarnational and counterpart selves upon this earth we know--but he also tells us that each of those selves can move into other or parallel realities. I quote him from Session 681 in Volume 1 of "Unknown" Reality:
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 6: Genetic Heritage and Reincarnational Predilections

quotes.text: "Any event, therefore, has an invisible thickness, a multidimensional basis. Your skies are filled with breezes, currents, clouds, sunlight, dust particles and so forth. The sky vaults above the entire planet. The invisible [vault of] Framework 2 contains endless patterns that change as, say, clouds do -- that mix and merge to form your psychological climate. Thoughts have what we will for now term electromagnetic properties. In those terms your thoughts mix and match with others in Framework 2, creating mass patterns that form the overall psychological basis behind world events. Again, however, Framework 2 is not neutral, but automatically inclined toward what we will here term good or constructive developments. It is a growth medium. Constructive or 'positive' feelings or thoughts are more easily materialized than 'negative' ones because they are in keeping with Framework 2's characteristics."
quotes.translation: "因此任何事件都具有无形的厚度、多维的基础。你们的天空充满微风、气流、云朵、阳光、尘埃粒子等等。苍穹笼罩整个行星。框架2的无形[穹顶]包含无穷模式，它们像云朵般变幻——混合交融形成你们的心理气候。思想具有我们暂且称为电磁特性的属性。按此说法，你们的思想在框架2中与其他思想混合匹配，形成集体模式，构成世界事件背后的整体心理基础。但再次强调，框架2并非中立，而是自动倾向于我们在此称为善或建设性的发展。它是成长媒介。建设性或'正面'的情感或思想比'负面'的更容易物质化，因为它们符合框架2的特性。"
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Seth speaks in Session 826, held for Mass Events on March 8, 1978:

quotes.text: I said that the inner ego reasons, but its reasoning is not restricted to the cause-and-effect limitations that you apply to the reasoning process. The action of the inner ego within the wider sphere of Framework 2 explains many events and seeming coincidences that otherwise seem to make no sense within your world. Many realities within Framework 2 cannot suitably be explained as facts to you in Framework 1, simply because they involve psychological thicknesses that cannot be translated into facts as you think of them. These often appear in the symbolic language of the arts instead, and many of your dreams are translations in which the events of Framework 2 appear in symbolic form.
quotes.translation: 我说过内在自我会推理，但其推理不受你们加诸推理过程的因果限制。内在自我在框架2更广阔领域中的行动，解释了那些在你们世界中看似毫无意义的许多事件和表面巧合。框架2中的许多实相无法作为事实向框架1中的你们恰当解释，仅仅因为它们涉及无法转化为你们所理解事实的心理厚度。这些往往以艺术的象征语言呈现，你们许多梦境就是框架2事件以象征形式出现的翻译。

quotes.text: I am speaking of that framework now only as it applies to your world -- not in its relationship to other realities. Earlier in his own experience Ruburt described that framework (in Psychic Politics) as the heroic dimension. He saw quite correctly that there was a great give-and-take between the two frameworks -- your regular working one, Framework 1, and this other more comprehensive reality. He did not thoroughly understand, however, the creative ramifications involved, for it did not occur to him at the time that the prime work of your world was actually done by you in that other wider aspect of your existence.
quotes.translation: 我现在谈论的只是这个框架适用于你们世界的部分——而非它与其他实相的关系。鲁柏早年在自身经验中（在《心灵政治》中）将那个框架描述为英雄维度。他正确看到两个框架之间存在巨大互动——你们常规运作的框架1与这个更全面的实相。但他当时并未彻底理解其中涉及的创造性分支，因为他未曾想到你们世界的主要工作实际上是由你们在自身存在的那个更广阔面向中完成的。

quotes.text: The more prosaic elements of the personality then take whatever measures seem necessary at the time, while new orientation is tried out. These methods may seem to lead to great distortions, particularly in contrast with the sensed possibilities of development. In one way or another, however, they still provide a framework in which the personality feels itself free to pursue its goals. The built-in impetuses provide clue points. When the new sensed reality is strong enough to provide not only greater comprehensions but also to construct a new framework, then the old framework is seen as limiting, and discarded.
quotes.translation: 人格中较为平淡的元素随后会采取当时看似必要的任何措施，同时尝试新的定向。这些方法可能导致巨大扭曲，尤其与感知到的发展可能性相比时。但无论如何，它们仍提供一个框架，让人格感到可以自由追求目标。内置的驱动力提供线索点。当新感知的实相足够强大，不仅能提供更深刻理解，还能构建新框架时，旧框架便被视为限制而被摒弃。

source_raw.title: (11:38. Now Jane came through with several pages of material for herself. Some of it was quite personal, but one part consisted of Seth's response to an assessment she'd written after supper tonight, wherein she examined her progress since the inauguration of her psychic abilities late in 1963. Seth, here, adds to our understanding of Jane's reactions to some of the challenges she took up 11 years ago. His material is also an extension of much that he gave in the 679th session for Volume 1 of "Unknown" Reality, when he discussed the early background of the probable Jane who chose to live in this physical reality, and how that Jane began to contend with her strongly mystical nature. See especially Note 8 for Session 679, and Appendix 1.)

quotes.text: Jung's collective unconscious was an attempt to give your world its psychological roots, but Jung1 could not perceive the clarity, organization, and deeper context in which that collective unconscious has its own existence. Reality as Framework 2 is organized in a different fashion than it is in the Framework 1 world, and the processes of reasoning are far quicker. In Framework 1 the reasoning processes work largely by deduction, and they must constantly check their own results against the seemingly concrete experience of physical events. The reasoning of the inner ego is involved with the creative invention of those experiences. It is involved with events in a context of a different kind, for it deals intimately with probabilities.
quotes.translation: 荣格的集体无意识试图为你们的世界赋予心理根源，但荣格未能洞悉集体无意识自身存在所依托的清晰性、组织性与更深层脉络。实相作为架构二，其组织方式与架构一世界截然不同，推理过程也迅捷得多。在架构一中，推理过程主要依靠演绎运作，且必须不断对照物理事件看似具体的经验来验证自身结果。内在自我的推理则参与这些经验的创造性发明。它在不同性质的脉络中与事件互动，因为它与可能性紧密交织。

quotes.text: "Again as with master events, we're dealing with a different framework of action entirely, where the Mona Lisa is 'more real' than the physical properties that compose it. This is not to deny the validity of its [materials]. But to discuss Seth and his ideas primarily from the true-or-false framework is the same thing as considering the Mona Lisa only from the validity of the physical properties of its paint and panel: very very limiting.... I don't have to 'live up' to anything. I don't have to 'make the material work,' or prove through my actions that it does, because it proves itself in the way that creativity does, by being beyond levels of true-false references. Otherwise I'm at cross-purposes with myself."
quotes.translation: "再次强调，正如主导事件一样，我们处理的完全是另一种行动架构——在此架构中，蒙娜丽莎比构成它的物理属性'更真实'。这并非否定其[材料]的有效性。但若主要从真假框架来讨论赛斯及其理念，就如同仅从颜料与画板的物理属性来评判蒙娜丽莎：极其局限……我无需'符合'任何标准。我无需'让材料奏效'，或通过行动证明其有效性，因为它以创造力自我证明的方式——超越真假参照的层次——自行彰显。否则我便在自相矛盾。"
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: One of those periods of delay in Jane's delivering the sessions for Mass Events lasted for 42 weeks, or 9½ months; in that book see the opening notes for Session 831, in Chapter 5. This hiatus in Chapter 9 of Dreams lasted for eight months, between sessions 928 and 931.

quotes.text: You can perhaps now see where this so-called astral body has its origin and purpose. It is actually the intangible but actual framework formed by inner energy, about whose lines or, actually, reference points, the physical framework is constructed. Now. This sort of elementary telepathy has existed as a necessity since the first formation of inner energy into matter. Some of our past sessions, particularly those explaining the initial appearance of matter, will set you clear, and you will see that the explanations I gave you then would have to involve such elementary telepathic communication between cells.
quotes.translation: 现在你或许能看清所谓星光体的起源与目的。它实则是内在能量形成的无形却真实的架构，物理架构正是沿着其脉络（更确切说是参照点）构建而成。这种基础心灵感应自内在能量首次凝聚为物质起便作为必需存在。我们过去的某些课，尤其是解释物质最初显现的那些，将为你厘清脉络——届时你会明白，我当时给出的解释必然涉及细胞间这种基础心灵感应的沟通。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: The scientific framework is basically, now, just as senseless, though within it the facts often seem to prove themselves out, also. There are viruses, for example. Your beliefs become self-evident realities. It would be impossible to discuss human suffering without taking that into consideration. Ideas are transmitted from generation to generation--and those ideas are the carriers of all of your reality, its joys and its agonies. Science, however, is all in all (underlined) a poor healer. The church's concepts at least gave suffering a kind of dignity: It did (underlined) come from God--an unwelcome gift, perhaps--but after all it was punishment handed out from a firm father for a child's own good.
quotes.translation: 科学框架本质上同样毫无意义，尽管其内部的事实往往也能自我印证。例如病毒的存在。你们的信念会变成不言自明的实相。若不考虑这一点，讨论人类苦难便绝无可能。观念代代相传——这些观念承载着你们全部实相中的欢愉与苦痛。然而科学终究（着重号）是蹩脚的疗愈者。教会的概念至少赋予苦难某种尊严：它确实（着重号）来自上帝——或许是不受欢迎的礼物——但终究是严父为子女好而降下的惩戒。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: As you have an ego, fully conscious, directed toward the physical world, you also have what I call an inner ego, directed toward inner reality. You have, in other words, a portion of yourself that is fully conscious in Framework 2. The ego in your ordinary world, which again we will call Framework 1, is uniquely equipped to deal with that environment. It manipulates with rules of cause and effect and consecutive moments. It deals with an objectified reality. It can stretch its capacities, becoming far more aware of inner events than it is normally allowed to do, but its main purpose is to deal with the world of effects, to encounter events.
quotes.translation: 正如你们拥有完全清醒、面向物质世界的自我，你们也拥有我所谓面向内在实相的内在自我。换言之，你们有一部分自我在架构二中完全清醒。你们寻常世界的自我（我们再次称之为架构一）具备独特能力处理该环境。它遵循因果律与连续时刻的规则运作，处理客观化的实相。它虽能扩展能力，比平常更敏锐地觉察内在事件，但其主要目的是处理结果的世界，遭遇事件。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Your Framework 1 life is, again, based on the idea that you have only so much energy, that you will wear out, and that a certain expenditure of energy will produce a given amount of work -- in other words, that applied effort of a certain kind will produce the best results. In the same way, it is believed that the energy of the universe will die out. All of this presupposes "the fact" that no new energy is inserted into the world. The source of the world would therefore seem no longer to exist, having worn itself out in the effort to produce physical phenomena. In the light of such thinking, Framework 2 would be an impossibility.
quotes.translation: 你们的架构一生活再次基于这样的观念：能量有限、人会耗损、特定能量消耗会产生固定工作量——换言之，某种特定类型的努力会带来最佳结果。同理，人们相信宇宙能量终将耗尽。所有这些都预设了"事实"：没有新能量注入世界。世界的源头因此看似已不复存在——在创造物理现象的过程中耗尽了自身。在此类思维下，架构二便成为不可能。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: (All very intent, leaning forward, eyes wide and dark:) In some manner, even a tragic composition of merit transcends tragedy itself. The composer was exultant in the midst of the deepest emotions of tragedy, or even of defeat. In such cases the tragedy itself is chosen as an emotional framework upon which the psyche plays. The framework is not thrust upon it, but indeed chosen precisely because of its own characteristics -- even those of despondency, perhaps.
quotes.translation: （所有人都非常专注，身体前倾，眼睛又大又黑：）从某种角度来看，即使是一部有价值的悲剧作品，也超越了悲剧本身。作曲家在悲剧或甚至失败的最深沉情感中，依然充满喜悦。在这种情况下，悲剧本身被选为一种情感框架，心灵在其中演奏。这个框架并非强加于心灵，而是恰恰因其自身特性——甚至可能是沮丧的特性——而被选择。

quotes.text: For the framework is the same framework in which you have your physical existence. We will make you investigators even in your sleep; and you can see now how your very investigations are bound to change the nature of your dreams themselves. Only in this case nothing will be lost, and much gained. It is unfortunate that medical men who deal with the mind as a specialty have not thought of some of these connections more thoroughly.
quotes.translation: 因为这个框架正是你们拥有物质存在的同一个框架。我们甚至会让你们在睡眠中成为调查者；现在你们可以看到，你们的调查本身必然会改变你们梦境的性质。只是在这种情况下，什么都不会失去，反而会收获很多。遗憾的是，那些专门研究心灵的医学人士，并没有更深入地思考这些关联。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: After death, for example, an individual may continue to create these -- masses of individuals may -- until they realize that the frameworks are no longer necessary. The Speakers were not confined in their activities, therefore, to waking consciousness. In all periods of your time they went about their duties both in the waking and sleep state. Much of the most pertinent information, in fact, was memorized by trainees during the dream condition, and passed on in the same manner. These unwritten manuscripts therefore were also illustrated, so to speak, by dream journeys or field trips into other kinds of reality. Such training still goes on. The particular psychic or story framework may vary. For example, conventional images of the Christian God and the saints may be utilized by the Speakers, with all of this highly vivid. The dreamer may find himself then in a magnificent harem, or instead in a brilliantly illuminated field or sky. Some Speakers confine their abilities to the dream state; and, waking, are largely unconscious of their own abilities or experience.
quotes.translation: 例如，死后，个体可能会继续创造这些——群体可能会——直到他们意识到这些框架不再必要。因此，传讯者的活动并不局限于清醒意识。在你们时间的各个时期，他们都在清醒和睡眠状态中履行自己的职责。事实上，许多最相关的信息都是在梦境中被受训者记住，并以同样的方式传递下去。因此，这些未成文的手稿，也可以说，通过梦境之旅或进入其他实相领域的实地考察而得到了说明。这种训练仍在进行。特定的心灵或故事框架可能会有所不同。例如，传讯者可能会利用基督教上帝和圣徒的传统形象，这一切都非常生动。做梦者可能会发现自己身处一个宏伟的后宫，或者在一个光芒四射的田野或天空中。有些传讯者将他们的能力局限于梦境状态；而在清醒时，他们对自己的能力或体验基本无意识。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Frameworks 1 and 2 obviously represent not only different kinds of reality in normal terms, but two different kinds of consciousness. To make this discussion as simple as possible for now, at least, think of these two frameworks or states of consciousness as being connected by "undifferentiated areas" in which sleep, dreaming, and certain trance states have their activity. Those undifferentiated areas are involved in the constant translation of one kind of consciousness into the other, and with energy transferences. You constantly process those data that come to you in your private life, and that information includes bulletins from all over the world, through your news broadcasts and so forth.
quotes.translation: 框架1和框架2显然不仅代表了通常意义上的不同实相，而且代表了两种不同的意识。为了尽可能简化目前的讨论，至少，请将这两个框架或意识状态视为由“未分化区域”连接，睡眠、做梦和某些出神状态在其中活动。这些未分化区域参与了一种意识向另一种意识的持续转换，以及能量转移。你们不断地处理那些在私人生活中来到你们面前的数据，这些信息包括来自世界各地的简报，通过你们的新闻广播等。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 823, FEBRUARY 27, 1978
9:43 P.M. MONDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: This is going to be a rather brief session comparatively. One note I wanted to make: As I have said, psychological time is a natural connective to the inner world. Though you experience days or hours within the framework of psychological time during the dream state, and yet do not age for a comparable amount of physical time, so as you develop in your use of psychological time you will be able to rest and be refreshed within the framework of psychological time while you are consciously awake. This will aid your mental and physical state to an amazing degree, and you will discover added vitality on the one hand, and a somewhat decreased need to sleep on the other.
quotes.translation: 相对而言，这将是一次相当简短的课。我想提一点：正如我所说，心理时间是通往内在世界的自然连接。尽管你们在梦境状态中，在心理时间的框架内体验了数天或数小时，但并没有在相应的物理时间内衰老，因此，随着你们在使用心理时间方面的发展，你们将能够在清醒状态下，在心理时间的框架内休息和恢复精力。这将极大地改善你们的身心状态，你们会发现一方面活力增加，另一方面睡眠需求略有减少。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: All of those characteristics have their sources in Framework 2, for the psychological medium in Framework 2 is automatically conducive to creativity. It is not simply a neutral dimension, therefore, but contains within itself an automatic predisposition toward the fulfillment of all patterns inherent within it. As James said in Ruburt's book,1 "The universe is of good intent." It is automatically predisposed, again, toward the creation of "good" events. I put the word "good" in quotes for now because of your misconceptions about the nature of good and evil, which we will discuss somewhat later.2
quotes.translation: 所有这些特性都源于框架2，因为框架2中的心理媒介自动有利于创造力。因此，它不仅仅是一个中性的维度，而是自身就包含了一种自动倾向，以实现其内在的所有模式。正如詹姆斯在鲁柏的书中所说：“宇宙怀有善意。”它再次自动倾向于创造“善”的事件。我现在把“善”这个词放在引号里，是因为你们对善恶本质的误解，我们稍后会讨论。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 825, MARCH 6, 1978
9:31 P.M. MONDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: Reasoning by itself can only deal with deductions made about the known world. It cannot accept knowledge that comes from "elsewhere," for such information will not fit in reason's categories, and confounds its cause-and-effect patterning. The power to reason comes from Framework 2. In the terms of this discussion, you are able to reason as a result of "magical" events that make reason itself possible. The term "magic" has in one way or another been used to simply describe events for which reason has no answer -- events that exist outside of the framework in which reason feels comfortable.
quotes.translation: 推理本身只能处理关于已知世界的推论。它无法接受来自"别处"的知识，因为这类信息不符合推理的范畴，并且会扰乱其因果模式。推理能力来自框架2。就本次讨论而言，你之所以能够推理，是源于那些使推理本身成为可能的"神奇"事件。"魔法"一词在某种程度上一直被用来简单描述那些推理无法解释的事件——这些事件存在于推理感到舒适的框架之外。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 825, MARCH 6, 1978
9:31 P.M. MONDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: The natural person is indeed the magical person, and you have both to some extent had very recent examples of such activity. You were, and are, trying to teach yourselves something. This is somewhat lengthy to unravel, but your behavior and experience, of course, is the result of your beliefs. Framework 22 has been a rather fascinating but mainly (underlined) hypothetical framework, in that neither of you have really been able to put it to any perceivable use in your terms. This is not to say it has not been operating. You have not had the kind of feedback, however, that you want.
quotes.translation: 自然的人确实是神奇的人，你们两人在某种程度上最近都有此类活动的实例。你们过去和现在都在试图教导自己一些东西。这需要相当篇幅来阐明，但你们的行为和体验当然是你们信念的结果。框架22一直是一个相当迷人但主要（强调）是假设性的框架，因为你们两人实际上都无法将其转化为你们所能感知的用途。这并非说它没有在运作。然而，你们没有获得你们想要的那种反馈。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: I do not speak through Ruburt as one might through a telephone. Instead there is a psychological extension, a projection of characteristics on both of our parts, and this I use for our communications. Later I will explain how this psychological framework is created and maintained, for it is like a road that must be kept clear of debris. You would be much better off in reading this book if you asked yourself who you are, rather than asked who I am, for you cannot understand what I am unless you understand the nature of personality and the characteristics of consciousness.
quotes.translation: 我并非像通过电话那样通过鲁柏说话。相反，存在一种心理延伸，一种我们双方特质的投射，我利用这一点进行我们的沟通。稍后我会解释这个心理框架是如何创建和维持的，因为它就像一条必须保持畅通的道路。如果你在阅读本书时问自己"你是谁"，而不是问"我是谁"，你会收获更大，因为除非你理解人格的本质和意识的特性，否则你无法理解我是什么。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: A certain portion of Adam was: the version was... You had a body consciousness of a protective nature, but this was only one portion of an entire personality who has yet to appear. Now there is a reason for the bridge framework. The bridge framework takes exercise, understanding, and knowledge on both of your parts, therefore there would be no communication of a strong valid nature until your personality is able to maintain it, for the bridge will not be strong enough to hold. Otherwise there would be too many implications and personality difficulties that could arise.
quotes.translation: 亚当的某一部分是：那个版本是……你拥有一种保护性质的身体意识，但这只是整个尚未显现人格的一部分。现在，桥梁框架的存在有其原因。桥梁框架需要你们双方付出努力、理解和知识，因此，在你们的人格能够维持它之前，不会有强烈而有效的沟通，因为桥梁还不够坚固。否则，可能会出现太多暗示和人格上的困难。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Any such communications coming through the inner senses will exist in your psychological time. I have said also that this psychological time operates during sleep and during quiet times of consciousness. Now in dreams and in the dream framework you have the feeling of experiencing many hours, or even days. These days or hours that you seem to experience in dreams are not recorded by the physical body, and are outside of your physical time camouflage. If in a dream for example you experience within the framework two days, physically you do not age for these two days.
quotes.translation: 任何通过内在感官传来的此类沟通都将存在于你的心理时间中。我也说过，这种心理时间在睡眠期间和意识平静的时刻运作。现在，在梦境和梦的框架中，你会有经历许多小时甚至许多天的感觉。你在梦中似乎经历的这些天或小时，并不被肉体所记录，并且存在于你的物理时间伪装之外。例如，如果你在梦中经历了框架内的两天，你的身体并不会因这两天而衰老。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: The framework is only for convenience, as your walls are for your convenience as I mentioned earlier. The walls are not there as such, but you had better act as if they were or suffer a possible broken neck. I must still respect many like frameworks in my own plane, but my understanding of them renders them less opaque--and that is a poor word--all the time.
quotes.translation: 框架只是为了方便起见，就像我之前提到的，你的墙壁也是为了方便。墙壁本身并不存在，但你最好表现得好像它们存在，否则可能会摔断脖子。在我自己的层面，我仍然必须尊重许多类似的框架，但我对它们的理解使它们变得不那么不透明——这个词用得不好——而且越来越如此。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: We will call the world as you physically experience it, Framework 1. In Framework 1, you watch television programs, for example. You have your choice of many channels. You have favorite programs. You follow certain scenes or actors. You watch all of these dramas, hardly understanding how it is that they appear on your screen to begin with. You are certain, however, that if you do buy a television set it will perform in an adequate fashion, whether or not you are familiar with electronics. Period.
quotes.translation: 我们将你们以肉身经验的世界称为架构一。在架构一中，举例来说，你们看电视节目。你们可以选择许多频道。你们有偏好的节目。你们追随特定的场景或演员。你们观看所有这些戏剧，却几乎不明白它们最初是如何出现在屏幕上的。然而，你们确信，只要买了电视机，无论是否熟悉电子学，它都会正常运作。句号。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 815, DECEMBER 17, 1977
9:22 P.M. SATURDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: You may have become a part of the drama of a natural disaster, or avoided it as a result of other seemingly chance occurrences. What appears to you as chance or coincidence, however, is actually the result of the amazing organizations and communications active in the psychological reality of Framework 2. Again, you form your reality -- but how? And how do private existences touch each other, resulting in world events? Before we go any further, then, we must look into the nature of Framework 2.
quotes.translation: 你可能已成为一场自然灾害戏剧的一部分，或因其他看似偶然的事件而避开它。然而，在你们看来是偶然或巧合的事，实际上是架构二的心理实相中活跃的惊人组织与沟通的结果。再说一次，你们形成自己的实相——但如何形成？私人存在又如何相互触及，导致世界事件的发生？因此，在我们进一步探讨之前，必须审视架构二的本质。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 823, FEBRUARY 27, 1978
9:43 P.M. MONDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: At the same time, because of the methods we use, some translation is often necessary. In many cases this translation is done by the intervening psychological framework, which is simply the psychological point closest to the meeting of Ruburts personality and my own, for he does not fade out as a personality. He extends himself in an expansion. The psychological expansion on his end, and the psychological expansion on my end, form the gestalt, the psychological bridge, of which I have spoken.
quotes.translation: 同时，由于我们使用的方法，常常需要某种翻译。在许多情况下，这种翻译是由介入的心理架构完成的，这架构正是最接近鲁柏人格与我人格交会点的心理点，因为他并未作为人格而消退。他扩展自己，进行一种扩张。他那一端的心理扩张与我这一端的心理扩张，形成了我说过的完形，即心理桥梁。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: Assembly-line time does not really value time -- only as time can be used for definite prescribed purposes. In that framework, to enjoy time becomes a weakness or a vice, and both of you to some extent have so considered time. With creative people strongly gifted, as in your cases, the natural person is very prominent, no matter what you do. It therefore strongly resents any basically meaningless constraints placed about its experience. It knows, for example, how to enjoy each day, how to collect creative insights from each and every encounter, how to enrich itself physically through household chores or other activities. It dislikes being told that it must work thus and so at command of unreasonable restraints.
quotes.translation: 流水线式的时间并不真正珍视时间——只将时间视为可用于特定规定目的之物。在那个架构中，享受时间成了弱点或恶习，而你们两人在某种程度上都如此看待时间。对于像你们这样天赋异禀的创作型人物，无论做什么，自然人格都非常突出。因此，它强烈憎恶任何强加于其体验之上的、基本无意义的约束。例如，它知道如何享受每一天，如何从每一次相遇中收集创作洞见，如何通过家务或其他活动在物质上丰富自己。它厌恶被告知必须按照不合理限制的命令如此这般地工作。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: I say wrong, meaning no moral judgment, but the application of one method to a pursuit that cannot be adequately expressed in such a fashion. The assembly-line time and the beliefs that go along with it have given you many benefits as a society, but it should not be forgotten that the entire framework was initially set up to cut down on impulses, creative thought, or any other activities that would lead to anything but the mindless repetition of one act after another (intently).
quotes.translation: 我说“错误”，并非道德评判，而是指将一种方法应用于无法以这种方式充分表达的目标。流水线式的时间及其伴随的信念，作为社会给你们带来了许多益处，但不应忘记，整个架构最初是为了削减冲动、创作思想或任何会导致除了无意识重复一个接一个动作之外的活动而设立的（专注地）。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You are social creatures, as the animals are. Despite many of your cherished, erroneous beliefs, your nations exist as the result of cooperation, not competition, as do all social groupings. To be ostracized is no laughing matter. The comfort of social discourse represents one of the great building blocks of families and civilizations. The set criteria of reality, therefore, operate as organizing, psychic, and physical frameworks. There is still more flexibility within those frameworks, however, than is recognized. You still try to carry your own cultural versions of reality into the dream state, for example, but the natural heritage of both body and mind escapes such repression -- and despite yourselves, in your dreams you come in touch with a greater picture of reality that will not be shunted aside.
quotes.translation: 你们是社会性生物，如同动物一样。尽管你们有许多珍视的错误信念，但你们国家的存在是合作而非竞争的结果，所有社会群体皆如此。被排斥绝非小事。社会交流的慰藉是家庭与文明的伟大基石之一。因此，实相的既定标准运作着，作为组织性的、心理性的与物质性的架构。然而，这些架构内的灵活性仍比人们所认知的更大。例如，你们仍试图将自己文化版本的实相带入梦境，但身体与心灵的自然传承却逃脱了这种压抑——而身不由己地，在梦中你们触及了实相更宏大的画面，这画面不会被搁置一旁。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (Session 757 was devoted to other matters that Jane and I were interested in.)

quotes.text: All being is manifestation of energy -- an emotional manifestation of energy. Man can interpret the weather in terms of air pressure and wind currents. He can look to fault lines in an effort to understand earthquakes. All of this works at a certain level, to a certain degree. Man's psyche, however, is emotionally not only a part of his physical environment, but intimately connected with all of nature's manifestations. Using the terms begun in the last chapter, I will say then that man's emotional identification with nature is a strongly-felt reality in Framework 2. And there we must look for the answers regarding man's relationship with nature. There in Framework 2 the nature of the psyche appears quite clearly, so that its sweeps and rhythms can be understood. The manifestations of physical energy follow emotional rhythms that cannot be ascertained with gadgets or instruments, however fine.
quotes.translation: 万有存在皆是能量的显化——一种情感性的能量显化。人类可以用气压和气流来解释天气，可以观察断层线来理解地震。这些方法在特定层面、特定程度上是有效的。然而，人类的心灵不仅在情感上是他物理环境的一部分，更与所有自然显化紧密相连。沿用上一章使用的术语，我要说：人类与自然的情感认同，在架构二中是一种强烈感受的现实。我们必须在那里寻找关于人类与自然关系的答案。在架构二中，心灵的本质清晰显现，其起伏与韵律得以被理解。物理能量的显化遵循着情感韵律，这些韵律无法被任何精巧的仪器或设备所测定。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: eth's statement at the end of dictation for the 815th session, "I will try to begin work on our book in a more predictable fashion," reflected his good intent, but things didn't turn out that way. Jane and I let the holiday season intervene to some extent, and Session 816, which came through the day after Christmas, didn't concern book work at all. Then, starting in early January, we held eight sessions having to do with questions we'd put off dealing with for a long time; some were personal and some were not. We stayed with our Monday-Saturday evening session routine, though, and kept busy with all of our other projects.1
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: They are the dreams that warn of famines or of wars. Such dreams, however, can also be triggered often, as in your own times, when the conscious mind is convinced that the survival of the species is threatened--and in such cases the dreams then actually represent man's fears. Overanxiety, then, can confuse the genetic system, and in a variety of ways. The existence of each of the species is dependent upon trust, indeed a biological optimism, in which each species feels the freedom to develop the potentials of its members in relative safety, within the natural frameworks of existence. Each species comes into being not merely feeling a natural built-in trust in its own validity, but is literally propelled by exuberance in its ability to cope with its environment. It knows that it is uniquely suited to its place within life's framework. The young of all species exhibit an unquenchable rambunctiousness. That rambunctiousness is built in.
quotes.translation: 它们是预警饥荒或战争的梦境。然而，这类梦境也常被触发——正如你们所处的时代——当意识心确信物种的生存受到威胁时，这些梦境实际上代表了人类的恐惧。过度焦虑会以多种方式扰乱基因系统。每个物种的存续都依赖于信任，一种生物性的乐观主义，在这种乐观中，每个物种都感到在存在的自然架构内，能够相对安全地发展其成员的潜能。每个物种诞生时，不仅对其自身有效性怀有与生俱来的信任，更被应对环境的丰沛能力所驱动。它知道自己独特地适应于生命架构中的位置。所有物种的幼崽都展现出不可遏制的活力。这种活力是与生俱来的。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Here in Dreams Seth uses the uncertainty principle as an analogy (and an excellent one), meaning that as the positions and motions of elementary particles, say, cannot be simultaneously measured precisely, so our genetic qualities and their motions can not always be specifically determined. In Dreams he's already said (in Session 909) that the human species has an "amazing interplay between genetic preciseness and genetic freedom," and (in Session 910) that "your genetic structure reacts to each thought that you have, to the state of your emotions, to your psychological climate." Choices and probabilities apply. Thus do we avoid genetic rigidity.

quotes.text: In a passionate, bloody series of events later in the seventh century, a split occurred in which the Moslem religion was divided into two main branches, the Shiite and the Sunni. Now Iran is ruled by the Shiites, and is religiously oriented; Iraq is ruled by the Sunnis, and is more secular and socialistic. Iranian leaders emphasize the religious aspects of the war, Iraq the ethnic. The rulers of each country have urged the citizens of the other to revolt against their leaders. There is much disillusionment in Iran over the excesses of the Shiite clergy. In Iran martyrdom is encouraged--at home, in the war, and in terroristic activity abroad. Iraq has been accused of using chemical warfare (courtesy of the Russians) against its enemy. The Moslem world, then, is hardly a monolithic entity; as within Iran itself, the myriad consciousnesses making up that whole framework are much too varied for that to be true.
quotes.translation: 在七世纪后期一系列激烈血腥的事件中，伊斯兰教分裂为两大主要分支：什叶派和逊尼派。如今伊朗由什叶派统治，倾向宗教导向；伊拉克由逊尼派统治，更为世俗化和社会主义化。伊朗领导人强调战争的宗教层面，伊拉克则强调民族层面。两国统治者都鼓动对方国家的人民反抗其领袖。伊朗国内对什叶派神职人员的过度行为深感幻灭。伊朗鼓励殉道——在国内、战争中以及海外恐怖活动中皆是如此。伊拉克被指控对敌人使用化学武器（承蒙俄国人提供）。因此，伊斯兰世界绝非铁板一块；正如伊朗本身，构成那个整体架构的无数意识太过多样，不可能如此单一。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The point is that past events grow. They are not finished. With that in mind, you can see that future lives are very difficult to explain from within your framework. A completed life in your terms is no more completed or done than any event. There is simply a cutoff point in your focus from your framework, but it is as artificial as, basically, perspective is applied to painting.
quotes.translation: 关键在于，过去的事件会成长。它们并未完结。明白这一点，你就会看到，从你们的架构内解释未来世是非常困难的。以你们的说法，一个完成的人生并不比任何事件更完整或结束。从你们的架构聚焦来看，只是有一个截断点，但本质上，这就像透视法应用于绘画一样是人为的。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (First, after holding session 805 we took a six-week break from sessions of any kind. We didn't plan to do this; it just developed, and we eventually realized that it did so through Jane's simple need for a change in routine. We had plenty of other things to do: I was still occupied daily with writing notes and appendixes for Volume 2 of "Unknown" Reality; on June 4 Jane received the page proofs for Cézanne, and began correcting them for the printer; on the 14th of the month "our" contractor began converting half of our garage into a writing room for Jane, and adding a large back porch [see the end of Note 2 for Session 801]. All of that building activity was much noisier and more disruptive than the work had been for the front porch, and forced some changes in our schedules, including more night work, as we manipulated around those distractions.
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: Now, separately, I sense a framework, as of a house that is not yet fully constructed. It may have to do with a young relative of his （pause）, or close friend, or someone connected with him. The house is on a hill. The framework is there but it is open, with the walls not yet constructed completely. （Pause.）
quotes.translation: 现在，我分别感知到一个架构，如同尚未完全建成的房屋。这可能与他的一位年轻亲戚（停顿）、密友或某个与他相关的人有关。房屋位于山丘上。架构存在但敞开着，墙壁尚未完全建好。（停顿）

quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: When that nature grew out of the framework, he left it. All the beliefs that had once seemed so legitimate were then seen as hampering, and all their defects became plain. While he followed the framework, nothing could swerve him from it, and here (touching the photo) in this child's picture, you already have the unswerving nature, the great spontaneity, looking for a structure that will allow it growth, and yet give the illusion of safety.
quotes.translation: 当那本性从架构中成长出来时，他便离开了它。所有曾经看似合理正当的信念，如今被视为阻碍，其缺陷变得显而易见。当他遵循那个架构时，没有任何事物能使他偏离；而在这张童年照片中（触碰照片），你已经能看到那坚定不移的本性、巨大的自发性，正在寻找一个既能允许其成长、又能给予安全幻觉的结构。

quotes.text: When he receives data in the form of images, the psychological framework is operating. On my end this framework projects imagery, and with my direction it uses Ruburt's own personal associations to direct his impressions toward the correct point. Then when we are successful I insert the correct data.
quotes.translation: 当他以影像形式接收数据时，心理架构正在运作。在我这一端，这个架构投射出影像，并在我的引导下，利用鲁柏的个人联想，将他的印象导向正确的焦点。当我们成功时，我便插入正确的数据。

quotes.text: "It is the adult's version of childhood knowledge, the human version of the animals' knowledge, the conscious version of 'unconscious' comprehension. I told you that Frameworks 1 and 2 were actually united. They seemed to be so disunited that it is almost impossible to discuss them using any other terms. To understand that much alone, to comprehend the simple idea of Framework 2's indisputable existence is strongly important, however."
quotes.translation: "这是成人版的童年知识，人类版的动物知识，意识版的'无意识'理解。我曾告诉过你，架构一和架构二实际上是统一的。它们看起来如此分离，以至于几乎不可能用其他术语来讨论。然而，仅理解这一点，仅领会架构二无可争议存在的简单概念，就极为重要。"

source_raw.title: 5. It was inevitable that Jane's images would remind me of the note I'd written well over two months ago, on re-creating the past, or updating it, through nostalgia. In Chapter 11 of Dreams, see Note 8 for Session 936. Her images led me to search out the collection of battered black-and-white snapshots of her that somehow, some way, she'd managed to save from her early childhood. Along with scraps of her youthful poetry, the pictures are the only physical remnants she possesses of her first years, and studying them anew I realized just how valuable they really are. I talked of having them copied and enlarged by a professional photographer; I speculated about eventually having some of them reproduced in a book. That idea may have to wait, however: For some years Jane hasn't cared to be photographed--or have pictures of herself shown, no matter when they had been taken.

quotes.text: "The natural person is indeed the magical person, and you have both to some extent had very recent (telepathic) examples of such activity.... Framework 2 has been a rather fascinating but mainly (underlined) hypothetical framework, in that neither of you have really been able to put it to any perceivable use in your terms. This is not to say that it has not been operating. You have not had the kind of feedback, however, that you want.
quotes.translation: "自然之人确实是神奇之人，你们两人在近期都曾有过（心灵感应）这类活动的实例……架构二一直是个相当迷人但主要（强调）假设性的架构，因为你们两人都未能真正将其转化为你们所能感知的用途。这并非说它没有运作。只是你们尚未获得想要的那种反馈。"
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: Basically, the psyche is a manifestation of pure energy in a particular form. It is very difficult to consider its experience outside of the framework familiar to you. You demand a certain preciseness of definition and terminology. That vocabulary automatically structures the information, of course. The psyche is a conglomeration of energy gestalts. To understand that, you must realize that pure energy has such transforming pattern-forming propensities that it always appears as its manifestations. It becomes its "camouflages."
quotes.translation: "基本上，心灵是纯粹能量以特定形式的显现。要思考它在你所熟悉的架构之外的体验非常困难。你要求定义和术语的精确性。当然，这种词汇会自动结构化信息。心灵是能量完形的集合体。要理解这一点，你必须认识到纯粹能量具有如此强大的转化模式形成倾向，以至于它总是以其显现形式出现。它变成了它的'伪装'。"
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Bridge perhaps is better. This psychological framework is in itself capable of growth, in terms of development. It represents on Ruburt's part a psychological expansion, and indeed on my own part also. It is formed partially by abilities inherent within all personalities, psychic abilities, and it is composed of energy. It is not a secondary personality, for it exists in quite a different dimension than secondary personalities.
quotes.translation: "或许'桥梁'更恰当。这个心理架构本身具有成长的能力，就发展而言。它在鲁柏方面代表一种心理扩展，实际上在我自己方面也是如此。它部分由所有人格内在的能力、心灵能力形成，并由能量构成。它不是次人格，因为它存在于与次人格截然不同的维度。"
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: Something in the nature of a fabric with a wooden framework I believe, for our object. The color blue. The framework is used for support. （40. second pause.） Upholstery. Blue upholstery, with something like small tacks in it that are indented. A blue chair. This is our object. （Pause.）
quotes.translation: "我相信我们的物品是一种带有木质框架的织物。颜色是蓝色。框架用于支撑。（停顿40秒。）软垫。蓝色软垫，上面有凹陷的小钉状物。一把蓝色椅子。这就是我们的物品。（停顿。）"
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: Unknowingly, the sick often give up their power to act in a healthy manner to the physicians. The doctors accept this mandate since they share the same framework of belief, so the medical profession obviously needs patients as badly as the ill need the hospitals. Society as you know it, not understanding the nature of normal aggression, considers it violent. The prisons and law enforcement agencies need criminals in the same way that criminals need them, for they operate within the same system of belief. Each accepts violence as a method of behavior and survival. (Pause.) If you do not understand that you create your own reality, then you may assign all good results to a personified god, and need the existence of a devil to explain the undesirable reality. So churches as they now exist in Western society need a devil as well as a god.
quotes.translation: 病人往往在不知不觉中将健康行动的力量让渡给医生。医生接受这种授权，因为他们共享相同的信念框架，因此医疗行业显然像病人需要医院一样迫切需要病人。你们所认知的社会，由于不了解正常攻击性的本质，将其视为暴力。监狱和执法机构需要罪犯，正如罪犯需要它们一样，因为他们在相同的信念体系内运作。双方都将暴力视为行为与生存的方式。（停顿。）如果你不了解自己创造了自己的实相，那么你可能会将所有好的结果归于人格化的神，并需要魔鬼的存在来解释不想要的实相。因此，西方社会现存的教会既需要神，也需要魔鬼。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: In those normal, generally accepted terms, the images in photographs do not change, move, or alter their relationships. The living subjective photography of dreams, however, provides a framework in which these "images" have their own mobility. They represent creativity in far different terms than you usually understand. You know what physical issue is (intently), because you see the children of your loins, but you do not experience the children of your dreams in the same physical way, nor understand that your dream life is continuous. It has organization on its own levels that you do not comprehend, and from its rich source you draw much of the energy with which you form your daily experience. Your conscious mind is the director of that experience.
quotes.translation: 在那些正常且普遍接受的术语中，照片中的影像不会改变、移动或改变它们的关系。然而，梦境中活生生的主观摄影却提供了一个框架，其中这些"影像"拥有自己的流动性。它们以远比你通常理解的更不同的术语代表创造力。你知道物质后代是什么（专注地），因为你看到自己亲生的孩子，但你不会以同样的物质方式体验你梦中的孩子，也不了解你的梦境生活是连续的。它在自己的层面上拥有你不理解的组织，并且从它丰富的源泉中，你汲取了许多用来形成日常体验的能量。你的意识心是那种体验的导演。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: So-called objective science gives you a picture, a model, that has served well enough in its own fashion, enabling you to travel to the moon, for example, and to advance in a technology that for a time you set your hearts upon. In the framework of objective science as it now exists, however, even the technology will come up against a stone wall. Even as a means, objective science is only helpful for a while, because it will constantly run up against deeper inner realities that are necessarily shunted aside and ignored simply because of its method and attitude.2 No objective science or splendid technology alone will keep even one man or woman alive, for example, if that individual has decided to leave the flesh, or finds no joy in daily life.
quotes.translation: 所谓的客观科学给你一幅图像、一个模型，它以其自身的方式已经足够好用，例如，使你能够登上月球，并在你们一度全心投入的技术上取得进步。然而，在现存的客观科学框架内，即使是技术也会撞上石墙。即使作为一种手段，客观科学也只是暂时有用，因为它会不断撞上更深的内在实相，而这些实相仅仅因其方法和态度就必然被搁置和忽略。例如，没有任何客观科学或卓越的技术能单独让一个男人或女人活下去，如果那个人已经决定离开肉身，或在日常生活中找不到喜悦。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: The people alive during any century are embarked upon certain overall challenges. These are the result of private challenges that can best be worked out within a certain kind of framework. Time as you understand it is utilized as a method of focus, a divider like a room divider, separating purposes instead of furniture. If you want a "Victorian room," you do not plank it down in the middle of a Spanish arrangement. Instead, you set it aside and frame it with its own decor, as you might in a museum that has separate rooms designating life in past centuries. The rooms in the museum exist at once. You may have to walk down a long corridor, go in a particular room and out the same door, before you can get to the next, adjoining room. The 18th-century drawing room may be next to a 12th-century chapel in this hypothetical museum, but you cannot move through one to the other. You have to go into the corridor first.
quotes.translation: 任何世纪中活着的人都投身于某些整体挑战。这些挑战是私人挑战的结果，而这些私人挑战最好能在某种框架内解决。你们所理解的时间被用作一种聚焦的方法，一个像房间隔板一样的分隔物，分隔的是目的而非家具。如果你想要一个"维多利亚时代的房间"，你不会把它放在西班牙式布置的中间。相反，你把它隔开，用其自身的装饰来框定它，就像在博物馆里那样，那里有分隔的房间来指定过去几个世纪的生活。博物馆里的房间同时存在。你可能必须走过一条长长的走廊，进入一个特定的房间，再从同一扇门出来，然后才能到达下一个相邻的房间。在这个假设的博物馆里，18世纪的客厅可能紧邻12世纪的小教堂，但你无法从一个直接走到另一个。你必须先进入走廊。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: ane and I consider Seth's concept of counterparts to be an intriguing psychological framework, spacious enough to serve as a workable thematic structure in which the social and nationalistic characteristics of our species can be studied, as well as the components of the individual psyche. That is, the private person is here seen as interacting with others because there is, beneath our awareness, an inner "person-to-person" relationship connecting each individual with his or her physical counterparts, though they may well be living in other parts of the globe while sharing the same historical period. It follows, then, that one may or may not ever meet a counterpart "in the flesh" -- may or may not even suspect the existence of such relationships.
quotes.translation: 珍和我认为赛斯的对应体概念是一个引人入胜的心理框架，它足够宽广，可以作为一个可行的主题结构，在其中可以研究我们物种的社会和民族特征，以及个体心灵的组成部分。也就是说，私人个体在此被视为与他人互动，因为在我们意识之下，存在着一种内在的"人对人"关系，将每个个体与他或她的物质对应体连接起来，尽管他们可能生活在全球其他地方，却共享同一历史时期。因此，一个人可能或可能不会"在肉身中"遇到对应体——可能或可能甚至怀疑这种关系的存在。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: Since you value sexual performance in the most limited of terms, and use that largely as a focus of identity, then both your old and young suffer consequences that are not so much the result of age as of sexual prejudice. It is interesting to note that both the old and the young also find themselves outside of your organizational frameworks. The young are more freewheeling in their thoughts before they accept sexual roles, and the old are more freewheeling in theirs because they have discarded their sexual roles. I did not say that old or young had no sexual expression -- but that both groups did not identify their identities with their sexual roles. There are of course exceptions. If the man or the woman is taught that identity is a matter of sexual performance, however, and that that performance must cease at a certain age, then the sense of identity can also begin to disintegrate. If children feel that identity is dependent upon such performance, then they will begin to perform as quickly as possible. They will squeeze their identity into sexual clothes, and the society will suffer because the great creative thrusts of growing intellect and intuitions will be divided at puberty, precisely when they are needed.
quotes.translation: 既然你们以最有限的术语来评价性表现，并主要将其用作身份认同的焦点，那么你们的老人和年轻人都承受着后果，这些后果与其说是年龄的结果，不如说是性别偏见的结果。有趣的是，老人和年轻人都发现自己处于你们的组织框架之外。年轻人在接受性别角色之前思想更自由奔放，而老年人则因抛弃了性别角色而思想更自由奔放。我并没有说老人或年轻人没有性表达——而是说这两个群体都不将他们的身份认同与性别角色等同。当然也有例外。如果男人或女人被教导身份认同是性表现的问题，并且那种表现必须在某个年龄停止，那么身份认同感也可能开始瓦解。如果孩子们觉得身份认同依赖于这种表现，那么他们就会尽快开始表现。他们会将身份认同挤进性别的衣服里，而社会将因此受苦，因为成长中的智力和直觉的巨大创造性推力将在青春期被分裂，恰恰是在最需要它们的时候。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: (11:41.) These strands are like double dreams that continue. They also serve as a framework to the recognized self. In periods of stress or challenge the recognized self may sense these other strains of consciousness, and realize that a fuller experience is possible, a greater psychological thickness. On some occasions in the dream state the recognized self may then enlarge its perception enough to take advantage of these other portions of its own identity. Double or triple dreams may represent such encounters at times. Consciousness always seeks the richest, most creative form, while ever maintaining its own integrity. The imagination, playing, the arts and dreaming, allow it to enrich its activities by providing feedback other than that received in the physical environment itself.
quotes.translation: (11:41分。)这些丝线如同持续的双重梦境。它们也为被认可的自己提供了一个架构。在压力或挑战时期，被认可的自己可能会感知到这些其他的意识流，并意识到更完整的体验是可能的，一种更大的心理厚度。有时在梦境状态中，被认可的自己会扩展其感知，足以利用自身身份的其他部分。双重或三重梦境有时可能代表这样的相遇。意识总是在寻求最丰富、最具创造性的形式，同时始终维持自身的完整性。想象力、游戏、艺术和做梦，通过提供除了物理环境本身接收到的反馈之外的反馈，使其能够丰富自身的活动。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

source_raw.title: (Later, Jane suggested that I ask Seth to comment upon the ideas in a session. There would also be a number of other reasons for double dreams, of course. I briefly wrote about them in Volume I of "Unknown" Reality; see the notes for Session 692.)

quotes.text: They cast their light over historical events because they are responsible for those events. They mix and merge the inner, unseen but felt, eternal psychic experience of man with the temporal events of his physical days, and form a combination that structures thoughts and beliefs from civilization to civilization. In Framework 2 the interior power of nature is ever-changing. The dreams, hopes, aspirations and fears of man interact in a constant motion that then forms the events of your world. That interaction includes not only man, of course, but the emotional reality of all earthly consciousnesses as well, from a microbe to a scholar, from a frog to a star. You interpret the phenomena of your world according to the mythic characteristics that you have accepted. You organize physical reality, then, through ideas. You use only those perceptions that serve to give those ideas validity. The physical body itself is quite capable of putting the world together in different fashions than the one that is familiar to you.
quotes.translation: 它们将光芒投射到历史事件上，因为它们对这些事件负责。它们将人类内在的、看不见但可感知的、永恒的心灵体验，与其物质岁月中的短暂事件混合并融合，形成一种组合，这种组合构建了从文明到文明的思想和信仰。在架构二中，自然的内在力量是不断变化的。人类的梦想、希望、渴望和恐惧在持续的运动中相互作用，然后形成你们世界的事件。当然，这种相互作用不仅包括人类，还包括所有地球意识的情感现实，从微生物到学者，从青蛙到星星。你们根据所接受的神话特征来解释你们世界的现象。然后，你们通过观念来组织物质现实。你们只使用那些有助于赋予这些观念有效性的感知。物质身体本身完全有能力以不同于你们所熟悉的方式将世界组合在一起。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: eth's statement at the end of dictation for the 815th session, "I will try to begin work on our book in a more predictable fashion," reflected his good intent, but things didn't turn out that way. Jane and I let the holiday season intervene to some extent, and Session 816, which came through the day after Christmas, didn't concern book work at all. Then, starting in early January, we held eight sessions having to do with questions we'd put off dealing with for a long time; some were personal and some were not. We stayed with our Monday-Saturday evening session routine, though, and kept busy with all of our other projects.1
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: Once scientists theorized the ether as the medium in which the physical universe existed.1 Framework 2 is the psychological medium in which the consciousness of the world exists. The word "ego" is much bandied about, and in many circles it has a poor reputation. It is, however, as I use it, a term meant to express the ordinarily conscious directive portion of the self. It is your conscious version of what you are -- an excellent description, if I do say so myself (with amusement). It is directed outward into the physical world. It is also aware, however, of some of your "unconscious" activities. It is the you you identify with, so it is as aware of your dreams, for example, as you are, and it is quite conscious of the fact that its existence rests upon knowledge that it does not itself possess.
quotes.translation: 曾经科学家们假设以太是物质宇宙存在的介质。架构二是世界意识存在的心理介质。"自我"这个词被广泛使用，在许多圈子里它名声不佳。然而，正如我所使用的，它是一个术语，旨在表达自我通常有意识的指导部分。它是你们对自己有意识的版本——如果我可以这么说的话，这是一个极好的描述（带着幽默）。它向外指向物质世界。然而，它也知道你们的一些"无意识"活动。它是你们所认同的那个你，所以例如它和你们一样知道你们的梦，并且它很清楚它的存在依赖于它自身并不拥有的知识。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In evolution man's nature is amoral, and anything goes for survival's sake. There is no possibility of any spiritual survival as far as most evolutionists are concerned. The fundamentalists would rather believe in man's inherent sinful nature, for at least their belief system provides for a framework in which he can be saved. Christ's message was that each man is good inherently, and is an individualized portion of the divine -- and yet a civilization based upon that precept has never been attempted. The vast social structures of Christianity were instead based upon man's "sinful" nature -- not the organizations and structures that might allow him to become good, or to obtain the goodness that Christ quite clearly perceived man already possessed.
quotes.translation: 在进化中，人类的本性是非道德的，为了生存可以不择手段。就大多数进化论者而言，没有任何灵性生存的可能性。基要主义者宁愿相信人类固有的有罪本性，因为至少他们的信仰体系提供了一个架构，在其中他可以得救。基督的信息是每个人天生是善的，并且是神性的个体化部分——然而，基于这一戒律的文明从未被尝试过。基督教庞大的社会结构反而是基于人类的"有罪"本性——而不是那些可能让他变得善良，或获得基督清楚看到人类已经拥有的善良的组织和结构。

quotes.text: Now give us a moment ... As creatures dwelling in time and space, your senses provide you with highly specific data, and with a cohesive-enough physical reality. Each person may react to the seasons in a very personalized manner, and yet you all share those natural events. They provide a framework for experience. It is up to the conscious mind to interpret sense events as clearly and concisely as possible. This allows for the necessary freedom of action for psychological and physical mobility. You are an imaginative species, and so the physical world is colored, charged, by your own imaginative projections, and powered by the great sweep of the emotions. But when you are confused or upset, it is an excellent idea to return your attention to the natural world as it appears at any given moment -- to sense its effect upon you as separate from your own projections.
quotes.translation: 现在给我们一点时间……作为存在于时间和空间中的生物，你们的感官为你们提供高度具体的数据，以及一个足够连贯的物质现实。每个人可能以非常个性化的方式对季节做出反应，然而你们都共享这些自然事件。它们为体验提供了一个架构。由有意识的心智尽可能清晰简洁地解释感官事件。这为心理和身体的移动性提供了必要的行动自由。你们是一个富有想象力的物种，因此物质世界被你们自己的想象投射所着色、充电，并被情感的宏大浪潮所驱动。但当你们感到困惑或不安时，将注意力返回到任何特定时刻的自然世界是一个极好的主意——去感受它作为独立于你们自己投射的对你们的影响。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: The universe expands, as I have said before, as an idea expands; and as sentences are built upon words, in your terms, and paragraphs upon sentences, and as each retains its own logic and continuity and evidence within that framework, so do all the portions of the universe appear to you also with the same cohesiveness (dash) -- meaning continuity and order. Any sentence is meaningful. It seems to fall in order by itself as you say it. Its order is obvious. That one sentence is (underlined) meaningful because of its organization of letters, or if it is spoken, its organization of vowels and syllables. It makes sense, however, not only because of the letters or vowels or syllables that are used within it, but because of all of the letters or vowels or syllables that it excludes.
quotes.translation: 宇宙在扩展，正如我之前所说，如同一个观念在扩展；并且正如句子建立在词语之上，在你们的说法中，段落建立在句子之上，并且每个句子都在那个架构内保留其自身的逻辑、连续性和证据，所以宇宙的所有部分也以同样的连贯性（破折号）向你们呈现——意味着连续性和秩序。任何句子都是有意义的。当你们说出它时，它似乎自然而然地按顺序排列。它的顺序是显而易见的。那个句子是有意义的（强调），是因为其字母的组织，或者如果是说出来的，是其元音和音节的组织。然而，它之所以有意义，不仅是因为其中使用的字母、元音或音节，还因为它所排除的所有字母、元音或音节。

quotes.text: "Now Ruburt had only one parent available most of the time, and he did not feel secure in that relationship--a situation chosen ahead of time, now. There is great leeway in the nature of such bondings.... With some people, they are so secure that they provide an overall, fairly permanent inner and outer framework. Ruburt's relationship with his mother left much to be desired. The bonding did not secure him that vital sense of safety, and he felt threatened by abandonment. His bonding to the cultural beliefs of religion was very strong to make up for that initial lack. The sinful-self material represents those ideas that were a strong element in his original belief structures. The 'troublesome' material remained relatively inactive until his curiosity and ability led him to actively challenge those ideas while [he was] also in a situation where the natural fear of abandonment might be suggested. At certain points, the assimilation of new information is so qualifiedly different from the original belief structure that in order to assimilate it the personality is left for a time between belief systems.
quotes.translation: 现在，鲁柏大部分时间只有一位父母可供依靠，而他在那段关系中并未感到安全——这是事先选择好的情境。这类情感联结的本质具有极大的弹性……对某些人而言，这种联结如此稳固，以至于能提供一种全面且相当持久的内外框架。鲁柏与母亲的关系远非理想。这种情感联结未能给予他至关重要的安全感，他始终被遗弃的恐惧所困扰。为弥补最初的情感缺失，他对宗教文化信仰的依附变得异常强烈。原罪自我的素材代表着他原始信念结构中的强势元素。这些"麻烦"的素材一直相对沉寂，直到他的好奇心与能力促使他积极挑战这些观念——同时恰逢可能引发自然遗弃恐惧的情境。在某些节点上，新信息的同化与原始信念结构存在本质差异，以至于为了消化这些信息，人格会暂时悬置于不同信念体系之间。

quotes.text: Dreams often serve as the frameworks in which sudden remarkable insights appear that later enable a man or a woman to envision the world in a way that was not earlier predictable. The world's activities always include the insertion of surprising events. This is true at all levels of nature, from microscopic to macroscopic. As I have said before, all systems are open. The theories of both evolutionists and creationists strongly suggest and reinforce beliefs in the consecutive nature of time, and in a universe that begins in such-and-such a fashion, continuing on to such-and-such an end--but there are horizontal events that appear in the true activity of nature, and there are horizontal entry points and exit points in all experience. These allow for the insertion of unofficial new energy, the introduction of surprising events. Period.
quotes.translation: 梦境常作为框架，让突如其来的深刻洞见得以显现，这些洞见随后使人们能够以先前无法预见的方式认知世界。世界活动始终包含意外事件的介入。这在自然界的各个层面——从微观到宏观——都真实不虚。如我先前所言，所有系统都是开放的。进化论者与创造论者的理论都强烈暗示并强化了时间连续性的信念，以及宇宙以某种方式起始、延续至某种终结的图景——但在自然的真实活动中存在着横向事件，所有经验中都存在横向的入口点与出口点。这些允许非官方的崭新能量介入，允许意外事件的发生。句号。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Unfortunately, the fundamentalists accept literal interpretations of intuitive realities in such a way that they further narrow the channels through which their psychic abilities can flow. The fundamental framework, in this period of time, for all of its fervor, is not rich -- as for example Christianity was in the past, with its numerous saints. It is instead a fanatical Puritan vein, peculiarly American in character, and restrictive rather than expansive, for the bursts of emotion are highly structured -- that is, the emotions are limited in most areas of life, permitted only an explosive religious expression under certain conditions, when they are not so much spontaneously expressed as suddenly released from the dam of usual repression.
quotes.translation: 不幸的是，原教旨主义者以如此方式对直觉实相进行字面解读，反而进一步收窄了其心灵能力流动的通道。在此时期，这种基本框架尽管充满热忱，却并不丰饶——不像过去的基督教那般拥有众多圣徒。它反而呈现为一种狂热的清教徒脉络，带有独特的美国特质，是限制性而非扩展性的，因为情感爆发被高度结构化——也就是说，情感在生活大多数领域受到限制，仅允许在特定条件下以爆炸性的宗教表达形式释放，此时它们并非自发流露，而是从惯常压抑的堤坝中突然泄出。

quotes.text: Before children are acquainted with conventional ideas of guilt and punishment, they realize that it is easier to bring about good events, through wishing, than it is to bring about unhappy ones. The child carries with him [or her] the impetus and supporting energy provided him at birth from Framework 2, and he knows intuitively that desires conducive to his development "happen" easier than those that are not. His natural impulses naturally lead him toward the development of his body and mind, and he is aware of a cushioning effect and support as he acts in accordance with those inner impulses. The child is innately honest. When he gets sick he intuitively knows the reason why, and he knows quite well that he brought about the illness.
quotes.translation: 在儿童熟悉传统的罪恶与惩罚观念之前，他们便意识到通过许愿带来好事比带来坏事更容易。孩童携带着出生时从框架二获得的动力与支持性能量，直觉地知晓有利于其发展的愿望比不利的愿望"更容易实现"。其自然冲动自然引导他们发展身心，当依照这些内在冲动行动时，他们能感受到缓冲效应与支持。孩童天生诚实。生病时他们直觉地知道原因，并且很清楚是自己导致了疾病。

quotes.text: In man, the probabilities of development are literally numberless. No computer could count the combinations of characteristics possible. It is highly important, then, that the species retain flexibility, and not become locked into any one pattern, however advantageous (intently)--and I am referring to physical or mental patterns. Within the framework of established specieshood, there must be every kind of leeway--leeways that are biologically activated, so that variances are constantly active. Those genetic variances may appear as defective or eccentric. They may appear as the handicapped. They may appear as superior characteristics of one kind or another, but they must be biologically stated as the variations from the genetic norm.
quotes.translation: 对人类而言，发展概率实际上不可胜数。没有计算机能计算可能特征组合的数量。因此，物种保持灵活性、不固着于任何单一模式（无论多么有利）至关重要——我指的是生理或心智模式。在既定物种性的框架内，必须存在各种弹性空间——这些弹性空间在生物学上被激活，使变异持续活跃。这些基因变异可能表现为缺陷或异常，可能呈现为残障，也可能表现为某种优越特性，但它们在生物学上必须被表述为偏离基因常态的变体。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In your terms, while you live, and in the most pertinent terms of intimate sensation, your reality must be what you perceive in the framework of your time, and what you create within that framework as it is experienced. Therefore, I entreat you not to behave as if man will destroy himself in some future -- not to behave as if man is an imbecile, doomed to extinction, a dimwitted, half-crazy animal with a brain gone amuck.
quotes.translation: 以你们的说法，在你们活着时，以最切身的亲密感受而言，你们的实相必然是在时间框架内所感知之物，以及在该框架被体验时于其中所创造之物。因此，我恳请你们不要表现得仿佛人类将在未来自我毁灭——不要表现得仿佛人类是注定灭绝的愚者，是心智迟钝、半疯狂的动物，拥有失控的大脑。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: New paragraph: Consciousness then is not dependent upon physical perception, though this attribute does require an awareness immersed within a material form. While physical consciousness is sifted through the bodily apparatus, you are usually unaware of noncorporeal kinds because of that process. The general framework, properties and characteristics of the body exist, therefore, before its formation. In simple terms, you choose ahead of time the kind of body you will inhabit and impress. It may seem to you that you do not have any conscious control over your body's condition in life as you know it, much less before your birth. You have been taught that there is little connection between your thought and your body's activities.
quotes.translation: 意识并不依赖于物质感知，尽管这种属性确实需要沉浸于物质形态中的觉知。当物质意识通过身体机制被筛选时，你通常无法察觉非肉体的意识种类，因为这种筛选过程的存在。因此，身体的一般架构、属性与特征在其形成之前就已存在。简单来说，你预先选择了将要居住并铭刻其上的身体类型。在你所知的此生中，你似乎对身体状况毫无意识控制，更遑论出生之前。你被教导认为思想与身体活动之间几乎没有关联。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Again, the writer or the artist also brings more into his work than the simple ability to write or paint. In one way or another all of his experience is involved. When you pay attention to Framework 1 primarily, it is as if you have learned to write simple sentences with one word neatly before the other. You have not really learned true expression. In your life you are writing sentences like "See Tommy run." Your mind is not really dealing with concepts but with the simple perception of objects, so that little imagination is involved. You can express the location of objects in space, and you can communicate to others in a similar fashion, confirming the physical, obvious properties that others also perceive.
quotes.translation: 再者，作家或艺术家带入作品中的，远不止写作或绘画的简单能力。无论以何种方式，他所有的经验都参与其中。当你主要关注架构一时，就如同你学会了写简单的句子，一个词工整地排在另一个词后面。你并未真正学会表达。在你的生命中，你写出的句子就像"看汤米跑"。你的心智并非真正处理概念，而是处理对物体的简单感知，因此几乎不涉及想象力。你能表达物体在空间中的位置，也能以类似方式与他人沟通，确认他人也能感知到的物理性、明显的特性。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: I also said that new entities were being formed, but in the framework of the spacious present all this is spontaneous. The contradiction seems a contradiction only on your terms. On my terms there is none. For practical purposes you may say, in truth on your own terms, that entities simply have had time to develop further personalities. But I want it understood that this is true only within your own time framework.
quotes.translation: 我也说过新的存有正在形成，但在广袤当下的架构中，这一切都是自发性的。这个矛盾只在你们的认知中才显得矛盾。在我的认知中并无矛盾。就实际目的而言，你们可以说——事实上以你们自己的说法——存有们只是有时间发展出更多人格。但我希望你们明白，这只有在你们自己的时间架构内才成立。
