quotes.text: Some people are naturally solitary. They want to live lone lives, and are content. Most, however, have a need for enduring, close relationships. These provide both a psychic and social framework for personal growth, understanding, and development. It is an easy enough matter to shout to the skies: "I love my fellow men," when on the other hand you form no strong, enduring relationship with others. It is easy to claim an equal love for all members of the species, but love itself requires an understanding that at your level of activity is based upon intimate experience. You cannot love someone you do not know -- not unless you water down the definition of love so much that it becomes meaningless.
quotes.translation: 有些人天生喜欢独处。他们想过孤独的生活，并且感到满足。然而，大多数人需要持久而亲密的关系。这些关系为个人成长、理解和发展提供了心灵与社会的架构。当你与他人没有建立强烈而持久的关系时，向天空高喊"我爱我的同胞"是件轻而易举的事。宣称平等地爱所有物种成员很容易，但爱本身需要理解，而在你们的活动层次上，这种理解基于亲密的经验。你无法爱一个你不认识的人——除非你把爱的定义稀释到毫无意义。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You may find some of your most cherished conceptions to be misconceptions in the light of your new experiences. Since explorations are highly personal, you will most likely begin them from the framework of your current beliefs. Symbols may be utilized, and these may change their meaning for you as you progress. The symbols may evolve, therefore. In the beginning of this work I "warned" the reader that here in these sessions we would go beyond ideas of one god and one self.3 I stated that your ideas of personhood would be expanded. As "Unknown" Reality is being produced Ruburt and Joseph are having their own experiences, and uncovering the nature of the unknown reality as it applies to them.
quotes.translation: 根据你新的经验，你可能会发现一些最珍视的观念其实是误解。由于探索极具个人性，你很可能从当前信念的架构开始。可能会运用象征，而这些象征在你进展过程中可能会改变其意义。因此，象征可能会演化。在这项工作的开始，我"警告"过读者，在这些课中，我们将超越单一神祇与单一自我的观念。我声明过你们对人格的观念将会扩展。当《未知的实相》正在被创作时，鲁柏和约瑟也在经历他们自己的体验，并揭示未知实相的本质如何适用于他们。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 725
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: The overall needs of the personality are always taken into consideration by what I have called in the past the inner ego. Now this portion of the self is indeed self-conscious in the highest meaning of the term--aware of the subconscious portion of the personality, aware of the primary conscious framework that you call your ego, and constantly directs the overall activities. This portion is aware of the complicated workings of the nervous system and all bodily functions. It is the overseer. It knows when to allow subconscious needs and wishes their fulfillment. It knows when to put fulfillment off for a time. It is this part of the personality that is in charge of overall stability.
quotes.translation: 人格的整体需求始终被我过去所称的内在自我所考量。这个自我部分确实是该术语最高意义上的自我意识——意识到人格的潜意识部分，意识到你称为自我的主要意识架构，并持续指导整体活动。这个部分意识到神经系统和所有身体功能的复杂运作。它是监督者。它知道何时允许潜意识的需求和愿望得到满足。它知道何时将满足推迟一段时间。正是人格的这一部分负责整体稳定性。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: When children dream, they utilize these inner senses as adults do, and then through dreaming they learn to translate such material into the precise framework of the exterior senses. Children's games are always "in the present" -- that is, they are immediately experienced, though the play events may involve the future or the past. The phrase "once upon a time" is strongly evocative and moving, even to adults, because children play with time in a way that adults have forgotten. If you want to sense the motion of your psyche, it is perhaps easiest to imagine a situation either in the past or the future, for this automatically moves your mental sense-perceptions in a new way.
quotes.translation: 当孩子做梦时，他们会像成人一样运用这些内在感官，然后通过梦境学会将这类资料转化为外在感官的精确框架。孩子的游戏总是"活在当下"——也就是说，他们立即体验当下，尽管游戏事件可能涉及未来或过去。"很久很久以前"这个短语具有强烈的唤起力和感染力，甚至对成人也是如此，因为孩子们以成人已遗忘的方式玩弄时间。如果你想感知自己心灵的运动，最容易的方式或许是想象一个过去或未来的情境，因为这会自动以新的方式调动你的心智感官知觉。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: While I am doing that I am also trying to introduce you, intuitively at least, to a larger framework, in which events straddle the reality that you know. Nevertheless, we will begin with issues in which it is very possible that contradictions may seem to occur, since your own definitions of an event are so simple that they ignore larger ramifications--ramifications that would reconcile any seeming contradictions in an overall greater unity of structure and action. Your imaginations will be of high value here, for they can often perceive unities that are not evident to the intellect--which you have trained to deal specifically with the evidence of the here and now.
quotes.translation: 当我这样做时，我也在试图至少凭直觉将你引入一个更大的框架，在这个框架中，事件跨越你所知的实相。尽管如此，我们将从那些很可能看似矛盾的问题开始，因为你对事件的定义过于简单，以至于忽略了更大的衍生后果——这些衍生后果会在一个整体更大的结构与行动统一体中调和任何看似矛盾之处。你的想象力在此将具有很高价值，因为它们常常能感知到理智无法明了的统一性——而你的理智已被训练得专门处理此时此地的证据。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Such effects may (underlined) appear suddenly within time's context, rather than slowly emerge, say, into that framework. It is, of course, that kind of outside-of-time activity that in your terms explains the origin of your universe. There are dimensions of activity, then, that do not appear within time's structure, and developments that happen quite naturally, following different laws of development than those you recognize. It is not just that highly accelerated versions of time can occur at other levels of actuality (long pause), but that there are dimensions in which those [versions] are no impediments to the natural "flow" (pause) of events into expression.
quotes.translation: 这类效应可能（下划线强调）突然出现在时间脉络中，而非缓慢地浮现于那个框架。当然，正是那种时间之外的活动，用你们的说法解释了你们宇宙的起源。因此，存在一些不在时间结构内显现的活动维度，以及遵循与你们所认知的不同发展法则而自然发生的发展。这不仅意味着高度加速的时间版本可以在其他实相层面发生（长停顿），而且存在一些维度，在这些维度中那些[版本]并不阻碍事件自然"流动"（停顿）进入表达。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Sometimes such cases are handled within another framework, in which Augustus would be considered possessed by an independent "evil" entity whenever Augustus Two took over. Now again, if Augustus somehow changed his beliefs it is possible that even within that framework some kind of cure would be effected. But at the same time the dangers and difficulties would make such a cure relatively impossible.
quotes.translation: 有时这类案例会在另一个框架内处理，在这个框架中，每当奥古斯都二号接管时，奥古斯都就会被视为被一个独立的"邪恶"实体附身。现在再说一次，如果奥古斯都 somehow 改变了他的信念，那么即使在这个框架内，某种疗愈也可能实现。但同时，危险和困难会使这种疗愈相对不可能。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: In your terms, birth defects of whatever kind are chosen before this life. This is done for many different reasons (just as people choose to be ill in this life, regardless of the duration involved). That is, a certain psychic framework is set up through which an individual decides "ahead of time" to experience an entire life situation. Some information on this has been given in my other writings.*
quotes.translation: 用你们的说法，无论何种出生缺陷都是在今生之前选择的。这样做有许多不同原因（正如人们选择在今生生病，无论持续时间长短）。也就是说，一个特定的心灵框架被建立起来，个体通过它"提前"决定体验整个人生情境。关于这一点的一些信息已在我其他著作中给出。*
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: They can impress others with greater import through their communications. They can move from inner to outer reality with easy ability. They know instinctively how to use symbolism. They are highly creative on an unconscious level, constantly forming psychic frameworks beneath normal consciousness that can be used both by themselves and others in dream and trance states. They often appear to others in the dream condition, and they help dreamers in the manipulation of inner reality. They form images with which the dreamers can relate, images that can be used as bridges and then as gateways into kinds of consciousness more separated from your own.
quotes.translation: 他们能通过自己的沟通以更大的重要性给他人留下印象。他们能轻松自如地从内在实相移动到外在实相。他们本能地知道如何运用象征。他们在无意识层面高度创造，不断在正常意识之下形成心灵框架，这些框架既可供他们自己也可供他人在梦境和出神状态中使用。他们常在梦境状态中出现在他人面前，并帮助做梦者操控内在实相。他们形成做梦者能关联的意象，这些意象可作为桥梁，进而作为通往与你自己的意识更分离的各类意识的门户。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You have set up the problem for yourselves within the framework of your reference. You will not understand your part within the framework of nature until you actually see yourselves in danger of tearing it apart. You will not destroy consciousness. You will not annihilate the consciousness of even one leaf, but in your context, if the problem were not solved, these would fade from your experience.
quotes.translation: 你们在自己的参考架构内为自己设定了问题。除非你们真正看到自己处于撕裂自然的危险中，否则你们不会理解自己在自然架构中的角色。你们不会摧毁意识，甚至不会消灭一片叶子的意识，但在你们的语境中，如果问题得不到解决，这些将从你们的经验中消失。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Such images are constructed of emotional energy, but they also are partially formed from the chemical structure of the individual or individuals behind such productions. The physical body then must work overtime. The impetus is emotional, and the pseudo framework is formed of emotional energy, but the image itself must be built up in part of physical components, and these must come from somewhere.
quotes.translation: 这类形象由情感能量构建，但也部分源自创造者个人的化学结构。身体因此必须超负荷运作。驱动力是情感性的，伪架构由情感能量形成，但形象本身必须部分由物质成分构成，而这些成分必须来自某处。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: The playfulness and creativity of dreams are vastly underrated in most dream studies. Children often frighten themselves on purpose through games, knowing the game's framework all the time. The bogeyman in the garden vanishes at the sound of the supper bell. The child returns to the safe universe of tea and biscuits. Dreams often serve the same purpose. Fears are encountered, but the dawn breaks. The dreamer awakes for breakfast. The fears, after all, are seen as groundless. This is not an explanation for all unpleasant dreams by any means, yet it is a reminder that not all such events are neurotic or indicative of future physical problems.
quotes.translation: 在大多数梦境研究中，梦的嬉戏性和创造力被严重低估。孩子们常常通过游戏故意吓唬自己，却始终知道游戏的架构。花园里的妖怪在晚餐铃响起时消失，孩子回到茶与饼干的安全宇宙。梦常服务于相同目的：恐惧被遭遇，但黎明破晓，做梦者醒来吃早餐，恐惧终究被视为毫无根据。这绝非对所有不愉快梦境的解释，但它提醒我们，并非所有此类事件都是神经质的，或预示着未来的身体问题。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The larger pattern of human personhood demands a bisexual affiliation that allows leeway in sexual encounters, a leeway that provides a framework in which individuals can express feelings, abilities, and characteristics that follow the natural inclines of the personal psyche rather than sexual stereotypes. I am not speaking here of anything so simple as merely allowing women more freedom, or relieving men from the conventional breadwinner's role. I am certainly not talking about "open marriage" as it is currently understood, but of far greater issues. Before we can consider these, however, there are several points I would like to make.
quotes.translation: 人类人格的更大模式要求一种双性取向，允许性际遇中的弹性空间——这个空间提供了一个架构，使个体能够表达遵循个人心灵自然倾向的感受、能力和特质，而非性别刻板印象。我在此并非谈论诸如仅仅给予女性更多自由，或让男性摆脱传统养家角色这样简单的事。我当然不是在谈论当前理解的"开放式婚姻"，而是远为重大的议题。然而，在探讨这些之前，我想先说明几点。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Your own main consciousness has the ability to travel faster than light (as noted at 9:37), but those perceptions are too fast, and the neurologically structured patterns that you accept cannot capture them. For that matter, cellular comprehension and reaction are too fast for you to follow. The poised framework of physical existence requires a particular platform of experience that you accept as valid and real. At that level only is the universe that you know experienced. That platform or focus is the result of the finest cooperation. Your own free consciousness and your body consciousness form an alliance that makes this possible.
quotes.translation: 你们的主要意识具有超光速旅行的能力（如9:37所述），但那些感知太快了，你们所接受的神经结构模式无法捕捉它们。就此而言，细胞的理解和反应也快得让你们无法追踪。物质存在的平衡架构需要一个特定的经验平台，你们将其视为有效且真实。只有在那个层面，你们所知的宇宙才被体验。那个平台或焦点是精妙合作的成果。你们自由的意识与身体意识形成联盟，使这成为可能。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (10:55: Jane's delivery had become somewhat faster and very absorbed as it progressed. Just as they had before our three-month layoff from book work [after Session 707], her trances for "Unknown" Reality were proving to be more "difficult" to initiate than those for the previous Seth books.9 She often had to wait for just the right moment to get back into dictation following a break, too; so tonight, after we'd shared an apple, she sat rather impatiently anticipating Seth's return. Resume, finally, at 11:25.)
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: This material should not make you feel unimportant or insignificant. The framework is so woven that each particle is dependent upon every other. The strength of one adds strength to all. The weakness of one weakens the whole. The energy of one recreates the whole. The striving of one increases the potentiality of everything that is, and this places great responsibility upon every consciousness.
quotes.translation: 这份资料不应让你们感到微不足道。架构编织得如此精密，每个粒子都依赖其他粒子。一方的力量增强全体，一方的虚弱削弱整体。一方的能量重创整体，一方的奋斗提升一切万有的潜能，这赋予每个意识巨大的责任。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: It is formed equally by those experiences which are purely subjective, and which exist only within the psychological time framework. The subjective experiences therefore result in definite changes within the physical body framework. These changes are not caused because of a physical event, but because of an event occurring within a dream condition which has no reality in your physical universe.
quotes.translation: 它同样由纯主观的体验构成，这些体验只存在于心理时间框架内。因此主观体验会在身体框架内引发明确变化。这些变化并非由物理事件造成，而是源于梦境中发生的事件——该事件在你们的物质宇宙中毫无真实性可言。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: On one level they could be said not to exist, and yet they do exist. While in your dream, you are able to see and touch and move about in these locations. It is only when you awake that they escape you. This should be considered along with our material on the expanding universe, since dream locations represent, certainly, a reality, even a framework that has no existence in your space; and measured purely along the lines used to measure your space, you would receive no hint at all of their existence or reality. Measured purely in terms of your camouflage conceptions, many things which you know to exist would seem not to exist.
quotes.translation: 在某个层面上，它们可以说并不存在，然而它们确实存在。当你们身处梦境时，能够看见、触摸并在这些地点移动。唯有醒来时它们才会消失。这点应结合我们关于宇宙膨胀的资料来思考，因为梦境地点无疑代表着某种实相，甚至是一个在你们空间里不存在的框架；若纯粹用测量你们空间的方法来衡量，你们完全无法察觉它们的存在或实相。若纯粹以你们的伪装概念来衡量，许多你们确知存在的事物反而会显得不存在。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: It is very possible for one dreamer who is a Speaker, to go to the aid of another individual who is having some difficulties in an inner reality within the dream state. The idea of guardian angels of course is highly connected here. A good Speaker is as effective within one reality as he is within the other, creating psychic frameworks within physical reality as well as within interior environments. Many artists, poets, and musicians are Speakers, translating one world in terms of another, forming psychic structures that exist in both with great vitality -- structures that may be perceived from more than one reality at once.
quotes.translation: 作为传讯人的做梦者，完全有可能去援助另一位在梦境内在实相中遭遇困境的个体。守护天使的概念与此高度相关。优秀的传讯人在一个实相中与在另一个实相中同样有效，既在物质实相中创造心灵框架，也在内在环境中创造。许多艺术家、诗人、音乐家都是传讯人，他们以一种世界诠释另一种世界，构建出在两个世界都充满活力的心灵结构——这些结构可同时被多个实相所感知。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: One is the cancer drive literature, and television "public service" announcements, in which the seven danger signals of cancer are given. Unfortunately, again, within the framework of your beliefs this also becomes almost a necessity for many -- especially for those who, because of previous experience of one kind or another with the disease, are almost irrational in their fear of it. The literature and announcements act as strong negative suggestions, following the nature of natural hypnosis -- as a conditioning process, you see, where you are looking for specific symptoms, and examining your body under the impetus of fear.
quotes.translation: 其一是癌症防治宣传文献和电视"公益广告"，列举了癌症的七大危险信号。不幸的是，在你们信念框架内，这对许多人而言几乎成为必需——尤其是那些因过往某种患病经历而对癌症怀有近乎非理性恐惧的人。这些文献和广告遵循自然催眠的本质，成为强烈的负面暗示——你们看，这就像条件反射过程，你们在恐惧驱使下寻找特定症状，检查身体。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The most full operation of the inner senses is possible only in this climate. There is so much here that I am impatient to tell you, and so little that you can understand now. Conceptions explode into being, evolve, change into something else, and yet all this within a framework that is definite but cannot be seen or touched by you, the psychological expansion even of atoms and molecules that in themselves contain condensed comprehensions beyond your understanding. The stuff of the universe is not inanimate, nor even on your plane is it an emptiness to be filled, but constantly expanding in ways that you cannot yet fathom.
quotes.translation: 内在感官最充分的运作唯有在此氛围中才可能实现。这里有太多我想告诉你们的内容，而你们目前能理解的却如此之少。概念迸发成形、演化、转化为其他形态，然而这一切都发生在明确却无法被你们看见或触摸的框架内——甚至原子和分子本身也包含着超越你们理解的浓缩认知，其心理扩张程度远超你们所能想象。宇宙的物质并非无生命，即使在你们层面也并非等待填充的空虚，而是以你们尚无法理解的方式持续扩张。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: On the other hand your intuitions follow a different kind of organization, as does your imagination -- one involved with associations, an organization that unifies diverse elements and brings even known events together in a kind of unity that is often innocent of the limitations dictated by cause and effect. In those terms, then, Framework 2 deals with associations, so that within it the recognizable events of the physical world can be put together in an infinite number of ways, after which they appear in your private experience according to directions you have given them through those associations that you form mentally.
quotes.translation: 另一方面，你们的直觉遵循着不同的组织方式，想象力亦然——这种组织方式涉及联想，能将不同元素统一起来，甚至将已知事件整合成一种不受因果律限制的完整性。就此而言，框架二处理的是联想，因此物质世界中可识别的事件能以无限方式在其中重组，随后根据你们通过心智形成的联想所赋予的方向，出现在你们的个人经验中。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: To some extent this is natural. You are focused in a daily life for a reason. You have adopted it as a challenge. But within its framework you are also meant to grow and develop, and to extend the limits of your consciousness. It is very difficult to admit that you are in many ways more effective and creative in the sleep state than the waking state, and somewhat shattering to admit that the dream body can indeed fly, defying both time and space. It is much easier to pretend that all such experiences are symbolic and not literal, to evolve complicated psychological theories, for example, to explain flying dreams.
quotes.translation: 这在一定程度上是自然的。你专注于日常生活自有其理由，你将其视为一种挑战。但在其框架内，你也注定要成长和发展，并扩展你意识的极限。承认自己在许多方面于睡眠状态比清醒状态更有效率、更具创造力是极其困难的，而承认梦体确实能飞翔、无视时空则更令人震惊。编造复杂心理学理论来解释飞翔梦境，假装所有这类体验都是象征性的而非字面意义的，要容易得多。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: From it is woven all material of your world and mine. If you consider the wires again, then you could view them as solidified emotion, woven together, however with a strong cohesive and stiffening power of the intellect. With feeling alone, although it is the basis, you would have an inconsistent, very precarious framework. Reason is the form that disciplines and upholds these frameworks.
quotes.translation: 你我的世界中的所有物质皆由它编织而成。若再次考虑那些线，你可将它们视为固化的情感，交织在一起，然而又带有理智的强烈凝聚与硬化力量。单凭情感，尽管它是基础，你将拥有一个不稳定、极不稳固的框架。理性是约束并支撑这些框架的形式。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: When you were both intensely involved in your projects, just finished, you let much of your inner experience slide, relatively speaking. The two of you operating together, however, then came up with an idea -- an important one -- that allows you to interpret the Framework 2 material in your own ways. You had instant feedback -- the interplay of a creative nature between the two of you involving your dreams and the camera,3 and so forth. You were each struck by the magical ease with which you seemed, certainly, to perceive and act upon information -- information that you did not even realize you possessed.
quotes.translation: 当你们两人都全神贯注于刚完成的计划时，相对而言，你们让许多内在体验溜走了。然而，你们两人共同运作，随后产生了一个重要的想法——一个让你们得以用自己的方式解读框架二资料的想法。你们获得了即时反馈——你们两人之间涉及梦境与相机等事物的创造性本质的相互作用。你们各自都惊叹于那种神奇般的轻松，仿佛你们确实能感知并依据信息行动——这些信息你们甚至未曾意识到自己拥有。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Those that do are chosen with great discrimination, dreams serving as one of the methods by which you ascertain the desirability of any given probable act. There is basically no difference at these other levels of existence between waking and dream events. Creatively, then, you organize your experience in such a fashion, with the conscious mind as you think of it also carrying its own responsibility. Those events that you do not accept as physical ones, however, also exist and join their own organizations. They do not simply fall away from your experience, but serve as focus points for events that do not concern you directly, while indirectly they form a definite psychological background. To a certain extent they become the invisible medium of experience from which your own specialized activities emerge, so that their nature is implied in your own life -- and so that your life is implied in those other frameworks.
quotes.translation: 那些被选中的人经过严格甄别，梦境是你们用以确定任何给定可能行为之可取性的方法之一。在这些其他存在层面上，清醒事件与梦境事件基本上没有区别。因此，从创造性的角度，你们以这种方式组织你们的体验，而你们所认为的意识心也承担着自身的责任。然而，那些你们不接受为物质事件的事件也同样存在，并加入它们自己的组织。它们并非简单地从你们的体验中消失，而是充当那些不直接涉及你们的事件的焦点，同时间接地形成一种明确的心理背景。在某种程度上，它们成为体验的无形媒介，你们专门化的活动从中浮现，因此它们的本质隐含在你们自己的生命中——而你们的生命也隐含在那些其他框架中。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now in many ways this applies to Ruburt also. The two of you seek refreshment together, then plunge into your inner realities. He is more Emotionally attuned than you, in that it is easier for him, when coming out of his private world, to rush toward you with spontaneity. You are both equally dependent however, basically, on this necessary Emotional refreshment. You both drink it in, and it sustains you for some period. It is used in your work, and in your joint work. It forms an inner framework of which you are sometimes aware. (Smile.) Without your feeling toward Ruburt and the resulting urge to Emotionally actualize yourself to another personality, your background could have prevented even artistic development. (Pause.) Rather than serve as a springboard for creativity.
quotes.translation: 现在，这在许多方面也适用于鲁柏。你们两人一同寻求更新，然后投入你们的内在实相。他比你更善于情感调谐，因为当他走出自己的私人世界时，更容易自发地冲向你。然而，你们两人基本上都同样依赖这种必要的情感更新。你们汲取它，它支撑你们一段时间。它被用于你们各自的工作以及共同的工作中。它形成一个你们有时能意识到的内在框架。（微笑。）若没有你对鲁柏的情感以及由此产生的在情感上向另一个人格实现自己的冲动，你的背景甚至可能阻碍艺术发展。（停顿。）而非成为创造力的跳板。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: No man or woman consciously knows for sure which day will be the last for him or her in this particular life, that each calls the present one. Mortality with its birth and death is the framework in which the soul, for now, is expressed in flesh. Birth and death contain between them the earthly experience that you perceive as happening within a given period of time, through various seasons, and involving unique perceptions within areas of space -- encountered with other human beings, all to one extent or another sharing with you events caused by the intersection of the self and time and space.
quotes.translation: 没有哪个男人或女人确切地知道，在此特定生命中哪一天将是自己或他人的最后一天，而每个人都称当下为“现在”。带有生与死的必死性，是灵魂此刻借以在肉身中表达的框架。生与死之间包含了你们所感知为在特定时间段内发生的尘世体验，历经各种季节，涉及空间区域内的独特感知——与其他人类相遇，他们都在某种程度上与你分享由自我与时空交汇所引发的事件。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: In that framework, even the emotions of love and exaltation are seen as no more than the erratic activity of neurons firing (pause), or of chemicals reacting to chemicals. Those beliefs alone bring on suffering. All of science, in your time, has been set up to promote beliefs that run in direct contradiction to the knowledge of man's heart. Science has, you have noted, denied emotional truth. It is not simply that science denies the validity of emotional experience, but that it has believed so firmly that knowledge can only be acquired from the outside, from observing the exterior of nature.
quotes.translation: 在那个架构中，就连爱与狂喜的情感也被视为不过是神经元放电（停顿）或化学物质相互反应的随机活动。光是这些信念就会带来痛苦。在你们时代，所有科学都被建立来推广与人类内心知识直接矛盾的信念。你已注意到，科学否认了情感的真实性。这不仅仅是科学否认情感体验的有效性，而是它如此坚信知识只能从外部获得，只能通过观察自然的外在表现来获取。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: (With emphasis, while Billy slept curled up against my left elbow as I sat on the couch writing down Seth's material:) Animals know that their own lives spell out life's meaning. They feel their relationship with all other forms of life. They know that their existences are vitally important in the framework of planetary existence. Beyond that, they identify themselves with the spirit of life within them so fully and so completely that to question its meaning would be inconceivable. Not inconceivable because such creatures cannot think, but because life's meaning is so self-evident to them.
quotes.translation: （强调语气，当比利蜷缩着靠在我左肘边睡觉时，我坐在沙发上写下赛斯资料：）动物知道自己的生命本身就诠释了生命的意义。它们感受到自己与所有其他生命形式的关系。它们知道自己的存在在地球存在的架构中至关重要。除此之外，它们如此完全而彻底地认同于内在的生命精神，以至于质疑其意义是不可想象的。不可想象不是因为这类生物无法思考，而是因为生命的意义对它们而言是如此不言自明。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Here in Dreams Seth uses the uncertainty principle as an analogy (and an excellent one), meaning that as the positions and motions of elementary particles, say, cannot be simultaneously measured precisely, so our genetic qualities and their motions can not always be specifically determined. In Dreams he's already said (in Session 909) that the human species has an "amazing interplay between genetic preciseness and genetic freedom," and (in Session 910) that "your genetic structure reacts to each thought that you have, to the state of your emotions, to your psychological climate." Choices and probabilities apply. Thus do we avoid genetic rigidity.

quotes.text: Basically the species is relatively so freewheeling, with so many potentials, that it is necessary that the mother's beliefs provide a kind of framework in the beginning, allowing the child to focus its abilities in desired directions. It knows ahead of time then the biological, spiritual, and social environment into which it is born. It is somewhat prepared to grow in a certain direction -- a direction that is applicable and suited to its conditions.
quotes.translation: 基本上，这个物种相对如此自由奔放，拥有如此多的潜能，以至于母亲在最初必须提供某种架构，让孩子能够将其能力聚焦于期望的方向。这样它就能预先知道自己出生的生物、灵性与社会环境。它多少已准备好朝某个特定方向成长——一个适用于并适合其状况的方向。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: All of them cast certain "atmospheric conditions" or reflections that color physical events as you know them. Some of your own feelings are propelled into a reality within such systems, adopting within that framework their own mass and form. In the creation and maintenance of your normal reality, you focus your daily waking consciousness so that it becomes effective within the ranges necessary. Ideas and feelings that you want made physical carry within them the mechanisms that will put them in the proper range, well within the electromagnetic field necessary for physical development.
quotes.translation: 它们全都投射出某种"大气条件"或反射，为你们所知的物理事件着色。你们的一些感受被推进这类系统内的实相，在该架构中采用自己的质量与形式。在创造与维持你们正常实相的过程中，你们聚焦日常清醒意识，使其在必要范围内变得有效。你们想要物质化的想法与感受，其内在携带的机制会将它们置于适当范围，完全处于物理发展所需的电磁场之内。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: For now think of it as you usually do, in a time context. It has been fashionable in the past to believe that each species was oriented selfishly toward its own survival. Period. Each was seen in competition with all other species. In that framework cooperation was simply a by-product of a primary drive toward survival. One species might use another, for instance. Species were thought to change, and "mutants" form, because of a previous alteration in the environment, to which any given species had to adjust or disappear. The motivating power was always projected outside* (underlined).
quotes.translation: 现在暂且按你们通常的方式，在时间脉络中思考它。过去曾流行一种信念，认为每个物种都自私地以自身生存为导向。句号。每个物种都被视为与其他所有物种竞争。在那个架构中，合作只是生存首要驱力的副产品。例如，一个物种可能利用另一个物种。物种被认为会改变，"突变体"会形成，是因为环境的先前改变，任何特定物种都必须适应或消失。动机力量总是被投射到外部*（下划线）。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 705
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: The events that you recognize as official have a unitary nature in time that precludes those probable versions of them, from which they arose -- versions that appeared to one extent or another in the dream state. Again, if you speak the English sentence "I am here," you cannot speak the Chinese version at the same time. In that regard, in your framework of action you choose to "speak" one event rather than another. Your formation of events, however, does not simply reside in your unique psychological properties, of course, but is possible because of the corporal alphabet of the flesh.
quotes.translation: 你们认定为官方的事件在时间中具有单一性质，排除了它们从中产生的那些可能版本——那些在梦境中以不同程度显现的版本。再说一次，如果你说英文句子"I am here"，你就无法同时说中文版本。就此而言，在你们的行动架构中，你选择"说出"一个事件而非另一个。然而，你们事件的形成当然不仅仅存在于你们独特的心理属性中，而是因为肉体字母表才成为可能。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: "Outside of that context, none of those fears make any sense at all. In a large regard the church through the centuries ruled through the use of fear far more than the use of love. It was precisely in the area of artistic expression that the inspirations might quickest leap through the applied dogmatic framework. The political nature of inspirational material of any kind was well understood by the church. Ruburt well knew even as a child that such religious structures had served their time, and his poetry provided a channel through which he could express his own views as he matured.
quotes.translation: 在那种语境之外，这些恐惧根本毫无意义。在很大程度上，教会几个世纪以来通过恐惧而非爱来进行统治。正是在艺术表达领域，灵感最可能迅速穿透僵化的教条框架。教会深知任何形式的灵感材料都具有政治性。鲁柏从小就清楚这类宗教结构已经过时，他的诗歌为他成熟过程中表达个人观点提供了渠道。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Seth's comments of February 17 also reflect upon Jane's own ideas as she described them just 11 days ago; see the excerpts in Note 4. Then in this Chapter 9 of Dreams, see Note 8 for Session 920.

quotes.text: This predictive picture is then set against two models. First it is checked against the body's ideal standard of health in its individual case -- its own greatest fulfillment. Then it is checked against the image of the body sent to it by the conscious self. Correlations are made instantaneously. In an organizational framework that would certainly be envied by the most advanced technological concern, communications spring back and forth with great rapidity. The body makes whatever changes are necessary in order to bring the two images in line with the present corporeal condition.
quotes.translation: 这个预测图像随后与两个模型进行比对。首先对照身体在个体案例中的理想健康标准——其自身最大的圆满状态。然后对照意识自我传递给它的身体意象。这些关联在瞬间完成。在一种连最先进的技术企业都会羡慕的组织框架中，信息以极快的速度来回传递。身体会做出任何必要的调整，使两个图像与当前肉体状况保持一致。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Molecular construction is formed from the inside, and is not rigid. On your plane such construction and such electronic and atomic patterns, frameworks and fields, are rigid to a degree, but even on your plane there is constant and apparent change. The pattern on your plane is more or less rigid while you exist on your plane. Nevertheless, the atoms and molecules within that pattern are far from rigid, though the pattern remains more or less the same. It is your habit, or the habit of your scientists, to carry apparent universal laws over into areas in which they do not apply.
quotes.translation: 分子结构由内而外形成，并非僵化不变。在你们的层面上，这种结构以及电子和原子模式、框架和场域在某种程度上是僵化的，但即使在你们的层面上，也存在着持续而明显的变化。当你们存在于你们的层面上时，这个模式或多或少是僵化的。然而，该模式内的原子和分子远非僵化，尽管模式本身大致保持不变。你们或你们的科学家习惯于将表面上的宇宙法则套用到不适用它们的领域。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: There are systems in which a moment,5 from your standpoint, is made to endure for the life of a universe. I do not mean that a moment is simply stretched, or that time is slowed down alone, but that all the experiences possible within a moment become realities within that framework. Such systems have little to do with you in any practical manner, nor is such information given to dwarf your idea of what your own consciousness is. It is important, however, that you realize the fact that there is more creativity and variety in an inner reality than you ever physically perceive.
quotes.translation: 存在一些系统，在其中，从你们的角度看，一个瞬间被延长到相当于一个宇宙的寿命。我并非指瞬间被简单拉长，或时间被单独减慢，而是指一个瞬间内所有可能的体验都在那个框架内成为现实。这类系统与你们在任何实际意义上都关系不大，提供这些信息也不是为了贬低你们对自身意识的理解。然而，重要的是你们要认识到，内在现实中存在着比你们在物质层面所能感知到的更多的创造力和多样性。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 682
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: Now. As I said earlier, sense data does have a reality, but this reality does not reside in an object. The object represents your interpretation of the basic reality. The energy belongs to the idea. In other words, the prime energy within physical reality resides precisely in those intangibles which do not, because of their nature, appear within physical frameworks. They give life and reality to the physical framework.
quotes.translation: 现在。正如我之前所说，感官数据确实具有真实性，但这种真实性并不存在于物体之中。物体代表了你们对基本现实的诠释。能量属于理念。换句话说，物质现实中的主要能量恰恰存在于那些因其本质而不会出现在物质框架内的无形之物中。它们赋予物质框架以生命和现实。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: The unknown reality and the psyche's greater existence cannot be separated from the intimate knowledge of the flesh, however, for the life of the flesh takes place within that framework. As earlier mentioned,4 the conscious self generally focuses in but one small dimension. Period. That dimension is experienced as fully as possible, its clear brilliance and exquisite focus possible only because you tune in to it and bring it to the forefront of your attention. In your terms, when you understand how to do this, then you can begin to tune in to other "stations" as well.
quotes.translation: 然而，未知的现实和心灵更广大的存在无法与肉身的亲密知识分离，因为肉身的生活就在那个框架内发生。如前所述，意识自我通常只聚焦于一个微小的维度。句号。这个维度被尽可能充分地体验，其清晰的璀璨和精妙的聚焦之所以可能，正是因为你们调谐到它并将其带到注意力的前沿。用你们的话说，当你们理解如何做到这一点时，你们就可以开始调谐到其他"频道"了。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (There were clear-cut connections between her creative performance today and her reception of the outline for The Way Toward Health last March; see the notes at 10:45, as well as Note 8, for Session 713; and in Volume 1, see Appendix 7. But there was even more creative expression to come through Jane this evening, not only in the session itself of course, but after it, as I try to explain in the concluding notes.
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: The idealizations themselves are made of "conscious" stuff. These are not inert data,1 then. The nature of probabilities determines the framework in which these fulfillments can take place, and "frames" living developments. The structure of probabilities provides on the one hand a system of barriers, in which practical growth is not chosen or significant; and on the other hand it insures a safe, creative, rich environment -- a reality -- in which the idealization can choose from an almost infinite variety of possible actions those best suited to its own fulfillment.
quotes.translation: 理想化本身是由"意识"材料构成的。这些并非惰性数据。概率的本质决定了这些实现得以发生的框架，并"框定"了生命的发展。概率结构一方面提供了屏障系统——在此系统中实际成长未被选择或失去意义；另一方面则确保了安全、丰饶、创造性的环境——一个现实——使理想化能从近乎无限的可能行动中，选择最适于自身实现的方式。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 697
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: The EE units are quite simply incipient forms of reality: seeds automatically given birth, suited for different environments, some appearing within the physical framework, and some not conforming at all to its prerequisites. Now some systems of reality are "bounded" with centers of faster-than-light particles. These begin to slow down at a rhythmic rate toward the peripheries, in your terms over great distances, until actually the outside slower particles to some extent imprison the center masses even though they move much more quickly, but within a confined area.
quotes.translation: EE单位简而言之就是现实的初始形态：自动诞生的种子，适应不同环境，有些出现在物理框架内，有些则完全不符合其先决条件。某些现实系统以超光速粒子中心为"边界"。这些粒子向边缘呈节奏性减速——以你们的尺度而言跨越巨大距离——直至外围较慢粒子在某种程度上囚禁了中心质量，尽管它们移动更快，却局限于受限区域。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Your daily life seems to give you little evidence of this. Your dreams, however, often contain this kind of interrelatedness. Because you perceive events in the way that you do, of course, you see the familiar physical universe. Dream events, not as precise in space and time, often serve as a framework through which some evidence of other universes can be glimpsed. No system is closed, so there are interactions, so to speak, between all universes. No psychological system is closed either, even while it retains an inviolate nature that is indestructible.
quotes.translation: 你的日常生活鲜少显现此理。然而梦境常蕴含这种相互关联性。因你以特定方式感知事件，自然得见熟悉的物质宇宙。梦境事件在时空上不甚精确，却常作为框架，使人得以瞥见其他宇宙的蛛丝马迹。没有系统是封闭的，因此所有宇宙间皆存在互动。心理系统亦然，即便它保有不可侵犯且不可摧毁的本质。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Your dreams are also uniquely yours, yet they happen within a shared context, an environment in which the dreams of the world occur. In that context your own existence is "forever" assured. You are the physical event of yourself put into a given space and time, and because of the conditions of that framework, within it you automatically exclude other experience of your own selfhood. The greater event of yourself exists in a context that is beyond your usual perception of events. That greater portion of yourself, however, forms the self that you know.
quotes.translation: 你的梦境亦独具个性，却发生于共享语境中——世界梦境发生的环境。在此语境中，你的存在被"永恒"确保。你是自身被置于特定时空的物理事件，因该框架的条件所限，你自动排除了对自我本体的其他体验。你更宏大的存在存在于超越常规事件感知的语境中。然而，那更宏大的自我部分，构成了你所知的自我。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In the same way you experience a birthday party, an automobile accident, a bridge game, or any psychological event as psychologically solid, with a smooth experienced surface that holds together in space and time. Such events, however, consist of indivisible "particles" and faster-than-light perceptions1 that never show. In other words, they contain psychic components that flow from Framework 2 into Framework 1.
quotes.translation: 同样地，你体验生日派对、车祸、桥牌游戏或任何心理事件时，视其为心理上的实体，具有在时空中凝聚的平滑经验表面。然而这类事件由不可分割的"粒子"与永不显现的超光速感知构成。换言之，它们包含从框架2流入框架1的心灵组件。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Christianity for many centuries served as an amazingly creative organizational framework, that expressed the vast complexity of the soul's reality. It also in its way managed to even focus some of man's less handsome attributes toward ends that were less reprehensible than in the past. Master events of that particular nature bring about a completely new interpretation of historic events. Their intensity, power, and seemingly impelling nature exist precisely because their origins are not physical, but are drawn from the psyche's deepest resources.
quotes.translation: 基督教在多个世纪中充当了惊人创造力的组织框架，表达了灵魂现实的浩瀚复杂性。它甚至以自身方式将人类某些不甚光彩的特质导向较过往更可宽恕的结局。此类特殊性质的主宰事件，带来对历史事件的全新诠释。其强度、力量及看似迫切的本质，正因其起源非物理性，而是源自心灵最深处资源。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: (Slowly:) In a world in which individuals were confined in space in a tribe or clan (a one-minute pause), action was immediate. The environment presented a framework in which consciousness learned to deal with stimuli in a direct fashion. It learned how to focus. The necessary specialization meant that only so much data could be handled at once, emotionally or otherwise. The formation of different tribes allowed man to behave cooperatively, in small numbers. This meant that those on the outside were selectively ignored, considered strangers.
quotes.translation: （缓慢地：）在一个个体被局限于部落或氏族空间的世界里（停顿一分钟），行动是即时的。环境提供了一个框架，意识在其中学会以直接方式处理刺激。它学会了如何聚焦。必要的专业化意味着无论情感上还是其他方面，一次只能处理有限的数据。不同部落的形成使人类能够以少量人数进行合作。这意味着外部的人被选择性忽视，被视为陌生人。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 686
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: As a continent does not exist alone, but also in relationship to other physical formations, so in your terms you form events so that they fit into a mass framework. You form your own reality. You do not form it isolated and alone, however. You are aware of other events, and take them into consideration, for example, regardless of appearances. You cannot force another person to experience an event he or she rejects. Nor can anyone act in like manner against you. So-called good or bad events each faithfully follow the inner mechanics.
quotes.translation: 正如一个大陆并非孤立存在，而是与其他物理形态相关联，在你们的术语中，你们形成事件使其适应一个整体框架。你们构建自己的实相。然而，你们并非孤立地构建它。你们意识到其他事件，并将其纳入考量，例如，不论表象如何。你们无法强迫他人经历其拒绝的事件。同样，也没有人能违背你们的意愿行事。所谓的好事或坏事都忠实地遵循内在机制。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: What gives life to chemicals now? That is the more proper question. All energy is (underlined) not only aware-ized but the source of all organizations of consciousness, and all physical forms. These represent frameworks of consciousness. (Long pause.) There was a day when the dreaming world, in your terms, suddenly awakened to full reality as far as physical materialization is concerned. The planet was visited by desire. There were ghost excursions there -- mental buildings, dream civilizations which then became actualized.
quotes.translation: 现在是什么赋予化学物质生命？这才是更恰当的问题。所有能量不仅（强调）被意识化，而且是一切意识组织与所有物理形态的源头。这些代表了意识的框架。（长停顿。）在你们的术语中，曾有一天，就物理物质化而言，梦境世界突然完全觉醒为真实。欲望造访了这颗星球。那里曾有幽灵般的探索——心灵建筑、梦境文明，随后它们被具体化。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

9:15 P.M. WEDNESDAY

quotes.text: All of an individual's experiences, even those of which he is not aware on a conscious basis, therefore are part of the electromagnetic reality that forms this particular individual's electromagnetic identity. It exists within the physical matter of the organism during existence within the physical system. While the experiences which form this framework and compose this individual's identity are obtained through his interaction with the physical system, his electromagnetic identity is not dependent upon the physical field.
quotes.translation: 因此，个体的所有经历，甚至那些他意识层面未察觉的部分，都是构成这个特定个体电磁身份的电磁实相的一部分。在物理系统内的存续期间，它存在于有机体的物理物质中。虽然构成这个框架并组成个体身份的经历是通过他与物理系统的互动获得的，但他的电磁身份并不依赖于物理场域。

quotes.text: The experience of any given unit, constantly changing, affects all other units ... Give us time ... It is difficult to explain because your concepts of selfhood are so limited ... These units contain within themselves, in your terms, all "latent" identities, but not in a predetermined fashion. Selves may be quite independent within the framework of their own reality, while still being a part of a larger reality in which their independence works not only for their own benefit, but for the sake of a greater structure.
quotes.translation: 任何给定单元的经历，不断变化，影响所有其他单元……给我们时间……这很难解释，因为你们对自我性的概念如此有限……在你们的术语中，这些单元自身包含所有“潜在”身份，但并非以预定方式。自我在其自身实相框架内可能相当独立，同时仍是一个更大实相的一部分，在这个更大实相中，它们的独立性不仅为自身利益服务，也为一个更伟大结构的目的而运作。

quotes.text: All of these procedures are unnecessary to consciousness itself, however. They are necessary to develop communication between nonphysical consciousness and the physical form which it has adopted. Consciousness, in forming an image, or creating it, then responds to it creatively, setting up frameworks for further creative actions. Consciousness experiences reality directly, but having formed physical matter into a personal image, it must then creatively translate data to that physical brain. It must keep it informed.
quotes.translation: 然而，所有这些程序对意识本身而言并非必要。它们对于发展非物理意识与其所采用的物理形态之间的沟通是必要的。意识在形成或创造意象后，会创造性地对其做出回应，为进一步的创造性行动建立框架。意识直接体验实相，但既然已将物理物质塑造成个人意象，它就必须创造性地将数据翻译给那个物理大脑。它必须保持大脑的信息更新。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: The intellect is brilliant, but on its own, now (underlined), it is indeed in its way isolated both in time and in space in a way that other portions of the personality are not. When it is overly stressed, with all of the usual frameworks or rationales that go along with it, it can indeed become frightened, paranoid, because it cannot really perceive events until they have already occurred. It does not know what will happen tomorrow, and since it is overly stressed, its paranoid tendencies can only fear the worst.
quotes.translation: 智力固然卓越，但就其本身而言，现在（强调），它确实在时间和空间上以人格其他部分所没有的方式孤立着。当它被过度强调，伴随着所有惯常的架构或理据时，它确实会变得恐惧、偏执，因为它无法真正感知事件，直到它们已经发生。它不知道明天会发生什么，既然它被过度强调，它的偏执倾向只会恐惧最坏的情况。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Seth speaks in Session 826, held for Mass Events on March 8, 1978:

quotes.text: When she sensed any strong feelings that Joseph also wanted such a home, then -- in your terms now -- she began, from her different framework after death, to bring that opportunity into his experience. This is not manipulation. It does show, however, that one portion of Joseph's mother, the portion connected to her son, still relates to him in a certain fashion. It also shows that his desires for a house in Sayre (deeper and stronger) helped bring about certain events: He could have such a house if he wanted one.
quotes.translation: 当她感觉到约瑟夫也渴望这样一个家的任何强烈情感时——用你们现在的话说——她开始从她死后的不同架构中，将那个机会带入他的经验中。这不是操控。然而，这确实表明约瑟夫母亲的一部分，即与儿子相连的那部分，仍以某种方式与他关联。这也表明他对塞尔的房子的渴望（更深、更强）促成了某些事件：如果他想要，他本可以拥有这样一栋房子。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: He thought of that realm as eternal and unchanging, a perfect but frozen composite that must indeed inspire men toward achievement on the one hand, and on the other reproach them for their failure, since their achievements must necessarily seem puny in contrast. Plato then saw Framework 2 as a splendid, absolute model in which all the works of man had their initial source. Man himself, according to this concept, could not affect that ideal world one whit. He could, however, use it as a source of inspiration.
quotes.translation: 他认为那个领域是永恒不变的，一个完美但冻结的复合体，一方面必然激励人类追求成就，另一方面又因他们的失败而责备他们，因为他们的成就相比之下必然显得微不足道。柏拉图于是将架构二视为一个辉煌的绝对模型，人类所有作品都源于此。根据这一概念，人类自身丝毫无法影响那个理想世界。然而，他可以用它作为灵感的来源。

quotes.text: In Framework 2 thoughts instantly form patterns. They are the "natural elements" in that psychological environment that mix, merge, and combine to form, if you will, the psychological cells, atoms, and molecules that compose events. In those terms, the physical events that you perceive or experience can be compared to "psychological objects" that appear to exist with a physical concreteness in space and time. Such events usually seem to begin somewhere in space and time, and clearly end there as well.
quotes.translation: 在架构二中，思想瞬间形成模式。它们是那个心理环境中的“自然元素”，混合、融合、结合，如果你们愿意，形成构成事件的心理细胞、原子和分子。用那些术语来说，你们感知或体验的物理事件可以比作“心理物体”，它们在空间和时间中似乎以物理的具体性存在。这类事件通常似乎始于空间和时间的某处，也明确地在那里结束。

quotes.text: In other words, that entire framework is meant to give you a standardized, mass-produced version of reality. None of its concepts can (knocking the table) rationally be applied to creative endeavors. The orientation that gives you the creative achievement lies in the opposite direction.
quotes.translation: 换句话说，整个架构旨在给你们一个标准化、批量生产的现实版本。它的任何概念都无法（敲桌子）理性地应用于创造性努力。赋予你们创造性成就的取向恰恰相反。

quotes.text: Whenever circumstances tend to revitalize such a charged emotion, that particular now will be re-created. The emotion will once again rush into the psychic space which is formed by elements in the personality's psychological environment. The immediate emotion of any moment, therefore, forms the framework of your present time within the waking state. As the emotion diminishes it takes up less of your psychic space, fills up less of your inner environment, and you seem to yourself to recede from it.
quotes.translation: 每当环境倾向于重振这样一种充满电荷的情感时，那个特定的当下将被重新创造。情感将再次涌入由人格心理环境中的元素形成的心理空间。因此，任何时刻的即时情感，在清醒状态下构成了你们当下时间的架构。随着情感减弱，它占据你们心理空间更少，填满你们内在环境更少，你们似乎从它那里退却。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: You know you can have more than one dream at a time. You can also experience versions of dreams of probable selves, but there will always be some point of contact--that is, there will always be a reason why you pick up such a dream. All of the dreams people have form a mass dream framework. Dreams exist at other levels, and physically of course they affect the body state. In such ways, the world's actions are worked out in mass dream communications that are at the same time public and private.
quotes.translation: 你知道自己可以同时拥有多个梦境。你也能体验可能自我的梦境版本，但总会存在某个接触点——也就是说，你之所以会接收到这样的梦境，必然有其原因。人们所有的梦境共同构成一个庞大的梦境架构。梦境存在于其他层面，当然也会在物质层面影响身体状态。通过这种方式，世界的行动在同时兼具公共性与私密性的大众梦境交流中得以展开。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: During our next session we will go more deeply into those pyramid gestalts of which we have spoken in the past, and perhaps consider from another viewpoint entirely the scope and nature that make up the idea of gods. Within certain frameworks these pyramid gestalts do have an electrical reality, but their existence goes beyond not the range but the nature of electrical reality. And because of the strength of their intensity, they are projected within every other field of actuality that exists.
quotes.translation: 在下节课中，我们将更深入地探讨过去提及的那些金字塔完形，或许会从完全不同的视角来审视构成"神祇"概念的范畴与本质。在某些架构内，这些金字塔完形确实具有电性实相，但其存在超越的并非电性实相的范围，而是其本质。由于它们强度的力量，这些完形被投射到所有存在的其他实相场域中。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: The full practical implication here should be understood: No course is irrevocably set or beyond change. Within the limited framework of your usual operations, however, so-called predictions3 may be made. They will be workable to some degree. In deeper terms however no action is set beyond alteration. The unknown reality is the source for the known one. If you want to "discover" how things work, then your journey must eventually lead you into the dimensions that lie within the world you know.
quotes.translation: 应当理解此处完整的实际含义：没有任何进程是绝对确定或不可改变的。然而，在你通常运作的有限架构内，可以做出所谓的预测。这些预测在某种程度上具有可行性。但就更深层而言，没有任何行动是固定不变的。未知实相是已知实相的源头。若你想"发现"事物运作的方式，那么你的旅程终将引领你进入已知世界之内的维度。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: Let the definition go for a moment however while I make this one point. I have said that our imaginary wires that seem to permeate our model universe are alive, and now if you will bear with me I will say that they are mental enzymes or solidified feelings, always of course in motion and yet permanent enough to form a more or less consistent framework. （Pause.） You could almost say that mental enzymes become the tentacles which form material, though I do not find that a very pleasant phrase.
quotes.translation: 暂且放下定义，容我说明这一点。我曾说过，那些看似贯穿我们模型宇宙的想象导线是活的，现在请容我进一步说明：它们是心灵酶或固化的感受，始终处于运动之中，却又足够持久，能形成某种相对一致的架构。（停顿）你几乎可以说心灵酶形成了构成物质的触须，尽管我不太喜欢这个说法。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: While you believe that only doctors can cure you, you had better go to them, because in the framework of your beliefs they are the only people who can help you. But the framework itself is limiting; and again, while you may be cured of one difficulty, you will only replace it with another as long as your beliefs cause you to have physical problems.
quotes.translation: 当你相信只有医生能治愈你时，最好去找他们，因为在你的信念架构中，他们是唯一能帮助你的人。但这个架构本身具有局限性；再者，即便你被治愈了某个病症，只要你的信念持续导致身体问题，你只会用另一个问题来替代它。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: In the distant past some ancient civilizations did indeed use animals in such a fashion, but in a far different framework. The doctors or priests humbly stated their problems verbally and through ritualistic dancing, and then requested the help of the animal -- so that the animals were not sacrificed, in those terms, nor taken advantage of. Instead, they united in a cooperative venture, in which animals and man both understood that no consciousness truly died but only changed its form.
quotes.translation: 在远古时期，某些古老文明确实以这种方式使用动物，但处于截然不同的架构中。医生或祭司们谦卑地通过语言和仪式舞蹈陈述问题，然后请求动物的协助——因此动物并非被牺牲（就那些术语而言），也不是被利用。相反，它们参与了一项合作事业，在此过程中动物与人类都明白：没有意识真正死亡，只是改变了形态。
quotes.book: The Way Toward Health
quotes.source_file: 10-10-10-10The Way Toward Health - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (I talked about the first session for Jane's symptoms -- the private portion of Session 208, for November 15, 1965 -- yet she said that for her the whole thing really started the day before we went to a party in June 1966 regarding How to Develop Your ESP Power. Our friends wanted to celebrate the publication of her first "psychic" book, her first mention of Seth, but she read poetry at the party and wouldn't talk about the ESP book -- too embarrassed. A strange, protective way to behave.

quotes.text: You must remember that you dwell always in a natural framework -- which means that your thoughts themselves are as natural, say, as the locks of your hair. In what may seem to you to be an odd analogy I will compare your thoughts with viruses,* for they are alive, always present, responsive, and possess their own kind of mobility. Physically speaking at least, thoughts are chemically propelled, and they travel through the universal body as viruses travel through your temporal form.
quotes.translation: 你必须记住，你始终栖身于一个自然的框架中——这意味着你的思想本身就像你的发丝一样自然。用一个在你看来或许古怪的比喻，我将把你的思想比作病毒*，因为它们具有生命、始终存在、能够回应，并拥有自身的移动性。至少从物理层面来说，思想由化学物质驱动，它们穿越宇宙之体，正如病毒穿越你暂时的形体。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Whatever the initial course of action agreed to in just this probable reality by everyone involved, from whatever point in the "past," in Framework 1 the participants have subjected it to an almost infinite variety of choices and modifications through the years: but always--always--within nature's great structure, and accompanied by the utter freedom of each person concerned to accept, reject, abort, or change the whole affair from their individual perspective at any moment....
quotes.translation: 无论最初行动方案在某个可能实相中由所有相关者如何商定，无论从"过去"的哪个时间点出发，在框架一中，参与者们多年来已将其置于近乎无限的选择与修正之中：但始终——始终——处于大自然的伟大结构之内，并伴随着每个相关者从自身视角随时接受、拒绝、中止或改变整个事件的绝对自由……
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: I don't really think we can conceive of anything to be truly "alien," though, so I use the word here only to lead into the next of Seth's larger concepts that I want to mention: that of probable realities, or probabilities, as Jane and I usually say. Not only does Seth stress the constant psychic motion of reincarnational and counterpart selves upon this earth we know--but he also tells us that each of those selves can move into other or parallel realities. I quote him from Session 681 in Volume 1 of "Unknown" Reality:
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 6: Genetic Heritage and Reincarnational Predilections

quotes.text: In such a situation, there are fulfillments to be gained from the parents' standpoints. There are always opportunities of growth and unusual creativity possible under those conditions for all involved. That is why the framework was chosen. The same applies to seeming tragedies such as accidents, or severe illnesses that come at any time.
quotes.translation: 在这种情况下，从父母的角度也能获得某种圆满。在这种条件下，所有相关者始终有机会获得成长与非凡的创造力。这正是该框架被选择的原因。这同样适用于看似悲剧的事件，比如随时可能发生的意外或重病。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: (The phone stopped ringing -- at last.) As contemporaries, counterparts choose a particular time framework. The time format alone makes certain focuses clear, that in your terms could not be made in another context. What you learn in your present about industry -- "progress" -- and the equitable sharing of the earth's products, could only be learned in a context in which industrialism was experienced as going too far, where technology was seen and known as a growing jeopardy.
quotes.translation: （电话终于不再响了。）作为同时代者，对应体选择特定的时间框架。仅凭时间格式就能凸显某些焦点，这些焦点在你们看来无法在其他情境中呈现。你们在当下关于工业——"进步"——以及地球产品公平共享的认知，只能通过体验工业主义过度发展、目睹技术成为日益增长的威胁这一情境才能获得。

quotes.text: You must know your own personality and develop your own abilities, and this should be your quest now. There is a strength of character, and indeed a rather heartless determination. These can be used to strengthen the framework and extend the limitations of your own personality. You are not developing your abilities, but using vital energies in pursuit of something that you do not basically want to attain.
quotes.translation: 你必须认识自己的个性并发展自身能力，这应是你当下的追求。有一种性格的力量，甚至近乎冷酷的决心。这些可用于强化框架并扩展你个性的局限。你并非在发展能力，而是在运用生命能量追求你根本不想达成的事物。

quotes.text: This is the ideal circumstance, for when this is the case then the ego listens to the inner self, and then directs its energy outward in a way that is beneficial for the whole gestalt framework. What you call negative suggestions are often judgments of the ego, for suggestion works from the ego to the subconscious, as well as it works the other way around. Such suggestions made by the ego can indeed be caught, and positive or constructive suggestions given to replace them.
quotes.translation: 这是理想状况，因为当此情形发生时，自我会聆听内在自己，然后以有益于整体完形框架的方式向外投射能量。你们所谓的负面暗示往往是自我的判断，因为暗示既从自我流向潜意识，也反向运作。自我发出的这类暗示确实可以被捕捉，并用正面或建设性的暗示取而代之。

I have experienced it frequently during psychological time since then. It is much richer and more varied than the more usual thrilling one notes upon a moving occasion. In the above instance, the initial source of the sensation appeared to stem from outside the body, and the waves against my ears had a tangible, solid quality that is difficult to describe.

quotes.text: During these years, then, Ruburt's position within his psyche has gradually shifted until he found a new, for him better, firmer point of basis. From this new framework he can more effectively handle different kinds of stimuli, and form these together to construct an understandable model of other realities. I will continue to speak from my own unique viewpoint, but in your terms Ruburt is one of you, and his explorations, taken from your perspective, can be most valuable.
quotes.translation: 在这些年里，鲁柏在心灵中的位置逐渐转移，直到他找到了一个新的、对他来说更好、更稳固的基点。从这个新架构中，他能更有效地处理各种刺激，并将它们整合起来，构建出对其他实相的可理解模型。我将继续从我独特的视角发言，但以你们的说法，鲁柏是你们中的一员，而他从你们的角度进行的探索，将极具价值。

quotes.text: Psychological time adds duration. You will find something else here. From the framework of psychological time you will see that clock time is as dreamlike and fleeting as you once thought inner time was. And you will discover that inner time is as much a reality as you once thought outer time was. You will discover your whole selves in other worlds, peeping inward and outward at the same time, and finding that all time is one time, and that all divisions are illusion.
quotes.translation: 心理时间增加了持续性。你会在这里发现别的东西。从心理时间的架构中，你会看到钟表时间就像你曾经认为内在时间是梦幻而短暂的一样。你会发现内在时间就像你曾经认为外在时间是真实的一样真实。你会在其他世界中发现自己完整的自我，同时向内和向外窥视，并发现所有时间都是同一时间，所有划分都是幻相。

quotes.text: Often in the aged you find such frameworks coming into being naturally, but those who awaken spontaneously after four hours consider themselves insomniacs because of their beliefs, and so cannot utilize their experience properly. Both the conscious and unconscious would operate far more effectively, however, under an abbreviated sleeping program, and for those involved in "creative" endeavors this kind of schedule would bring greater intuition and applied knowledge.
quotes.translation: 在老年人中，你常会发现这样的架构自然形成，但那些在四小时后自然醒来的人，由于他们的信念，会认为自己失眠，从而无法妥善利用他们的体验。然而，在缩短的睡眠计划下，意识和潜意识都会更有效地运作，而对于从事“创造性”工作的人来说，这种作息会带来更强的直觉和应用知识。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: For a start you will acknowledge your existence in the framework of nature, and to do that you must recognize the vast cooperative processes that connect each species with each other one. If you truly use your prerogatives as an individual in your country, then you can exert far more power in normal daily living than you do now. Every time you affirm the rightness of your own existence, you help others. Your mental states are part of the planet's psychic atmosphere.
quotes.translation: 首先，你会承认自己在自然架构中的存在，要做到这一点，你必须认识到连接每个物种的广阔合作过程。如果你真正运用自己作为国家中个体的特权，那么你在日常生活中的力量将远超现在。每当你肯定自己存在的正当性，你就在帮助他人。你的心理状态是地球心灵氛围的一部分。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Such action serves as a safeguard so that you do not overemphasize the gaps that may exist in yourself or in society, between the reality and the ideal condition. Many people want to change the world for the better, but that ideal seems so awe-inspiring that they think they can make no headway unless they perform some great acts of daring or heroism, or envision themselves in some political or religious place of power, or promote an uprising or rebellion. The ideal seems so remote and unreachable that, again, sometimes any means, however reprehensible, eventually can seem justified (see Session 850, for example). To change the world for the better, you must begin by changing your own life. There is no other way.
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: Our sessions still will follow many developments, and in some later years when you have also learned more, there will be channels opened of direct experience within the session framework. Behind the symbol of language is essence. Knowledge must be given to you in such a way that it makes sensewithin your particular framework.
quotes.translation: 我们的课程仍将跟随许多发展，在未来的某些年份，当你们学到更多时，在课程架构内将打开直接体验的通道。在语言的象征背后是本质。知识必须以在你们特定架构内具有意义的方式给予你们。

quotes.text: I am well aware that the book raises many more questions than it presents answers for, and this has been my intent. The unknown reality will become known to the extent that you form new questions, and forget the old frameworks in which answers and myths were automatically given in response. If this book "works," then many old questions will be seen as relatively meaningless, formed not after any intimate encounter with basic issues, but in response to old dogmas.
quotes.translation: 我很清楚这本书提出的问题远多于它给出的答案，而这正是我的意图。未知的实相将随着你们提出新问题而变得可知，并忘记那些自动给出答案和神话的旧架构。如果这本书“奏效”，那么许多旧问题将被视为相对无意义，它们并非源于对基本问题的亲密接触，而是对旧教条的反应。

quotes.text: There are no limits to the types of projections that can occur, basically speaking, for there are no limitations to the self, and a projection is an extension of the self. Your present existence is of course a projection. Activities based upon the framework of your present existence must be initiated within a system of chemicals and physical properties which make physical existence possible.
quotes.translation: 从根本而言，可能发生的投射类型没有限制，因为自体本无局限，而投射正是自体的延伸。你当前的存在当然也是一种投射。基于你当前存在框架的活动，必须在使物质存在成为可能的化学与物理属性系统内启动。

quotes.text: Now. Although you have chosen to form a particular group of qualities into a field pattern of a particular personality, upon which you focus the bulk of your energy, there are also other more shadowy, less well-constructed possibilities of personality selves that exist loosely within the psychic framework of the dominant personality, and these also have their influence. They also attempt physical constructions to a limited degree, and with limited success.
quotes.translation: 现在。虽然你选择将特定品质组合成特定人格的场域模式，并将大部分能量聚焦于此，但在主导人格的心灵框架内，还松散存在着其他更模糊、建构较不完整的人格自体可能性，这些可能性同样具有影响力。它们也在有限程度上尝试物质建构，并取得有限的成功。

quotes.text: These so-called real events, however, have changed radically through the ages. "Once" the gods walked the earth, and waged battles in the skies and seas. People who believed such things were considered sane -- and were sane, for the accepted framework of events was far different from your own. In historic terms the changing nature of accepted events provides far more than, say, a history of civilization, but mirrors the ever-creative nature of the psyche.
quotes.translation: 然而，这些所谓真实事件历经时代已发生根本性改变。"曾经"众神行走大地，在天空与海洋中征战。相信此类事物的人被视为心智健全——他们确实心智健全，因为当时被接受的事件框架与你们截然不同。从历史角度看，被接受事件不断变化的本质，不仅提供了文明史之类的记载，更映照出心灵永具创造性的本质。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Man is so highly verbal that he finds it difficult to understand that other species work with idea-complexes (with a hyphen) of a different kind, in which of course thought as you consider it is not involved. But an equivalent exists; using an analogy, it is as if ideas are built up not through sentence structure reinforced by inner visual images, but by like "mental" patterns structured through touch and scent -- in other words, thinking, but within a framework entirely different and alien to you.
quotes.translation: 人类过于依赖语言，以致难以理解其他物种运用不同种类的"观念复合体"（带连字符），其中自然不涉及你们所认为的思想。但存在对应物；打个比方，观念并非通过内在视觉影像强化的句子结构来构建，而是通过触觉与嗅觉构建的类似"心智"模式——换言之，这是思考，但处于与你们完全相异且陌生的框架内。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: I told you that we do not experience your time sequence. We travel through various intensities. Our work, development, and experience all takes place within what I term the "moment point." Here, within the moment point, the smallest thought is brought to fruition, the slightest possibility explored, the probabilities thoroughly examined, the least or the most forceful feeling entertained. It is difficult to explain this clearly, and yet the moment point is the framework within which we have our psychological experience. Within it, simultaneous actions follow "freely" through associative patterns. For example, pretend that I think of you, Joseph. In so doing I immediately experience -- and fully -- your past, present, and future (in your terms), and all of those strong or determining emotions and motivations that have ruled you. I can travel through those experiences with you, if I choose. We can follow a consciousness through all of its forms, for example, and in your terms, within the flicker of an eye.
quotes.translation: 我告诉过你们，我们不体验你们的时间序列。我们穿越各种强度。我们的工作、发展与体验都发生在我所称的"瞬间点"内。在此，在瞬间点内，最微小的念头得以实现，最细微的可能性得以探索，或然率被彻底检视，最微弱或最强烈的情感都被容纳。这很难清晰解释，但瞬间点正是我们拥有心理体验的框架。在其中，同时发生的行动通过联想模式"自由"延续。例如，假设我想起你，约瑟夫。如此我便立即——且完整地——体验你的过去、现在与未来（以你们的时间观），以及所有支配你的强烈或决定性情绪与动机。若我选择，便能与你一同穿越那些体验。例如，我们可以跟随意识穿越其所有形态，而按你们的时间观，这发生在眨眼之间。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Because of the ego's particular line of development, you have experimented with artificial drugs and chemicals, both in foods and for medicinal purposes, as well as for "religious" enlightenment. Some of the effects of LSD7 and other artificial psychedelic drugs give you a hint of other probable directions your consciousness might have followed, or might still follow. As the experiments are conducted, however, and in the ignorance of the framework, the conscious mind takes a subordinate position. Instead, using methods other than drugs, it could be taught to expand its knowledge far more safely, to organize it in ways that could be most advantageous. Still, some of the experiments do give hints of certain aspects of one of the species' probable developments.
quotes.translation: 由于自我特定的发展路线，你们曾实验人工药物与化学制品，既用于食物与医疗，也用于"宗教"启迪。LSD7及其他人工迷幻药物的某些效果，暗示了你们意识可能遵循或仍可遵循的其他可能方向。然而，在这些实验进行时，由于对框架的无知，意识心智处于从属地位。若能采用药物之外的方法，它本可被教导以更安全的方式扩展知识，以最有利的方式组织知识。不过，某些实验确实暗示了该物种可能发展的某些面向。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: In your terms, until now your consciousness has specialized in neurological patterning. As mentioned (in Session 682), this was extremely important while it learned the art of specialized focus. Now, however, it must begin to recognize that it can indeed expand, and bring into its awareness other quite legitimate realities. The nature of probabilities must be understood, for the time has come in the world as you experience it where the greatest wisdom and discrimination are needed. Your consciousness and neurological prejudice blind you to the full dimension of physical activity. The true implications of physical action are not as yet apparent to you.
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: Now: Take another photograph of yourself at a different age than the first one you chose. Ask yourself simply: "Am I looking at the same person?" How familiar or how strange is this second photograph? How does it differ from the first one you picked this evening? What similarities are there that unite both photographs in your mind? What experiences did you have when each photograph was taken? What ways did you think of following in one picture that were not followed in the other one? Those directions were pursued. If they were not pursued by the self you recognize, then they were by a self that is probable in your terms. In your mind follow what directions that self would have taken, as you think of such events. If you find a line of development that you now wish you had pursued, but had not, then think deeply about the ways in which those activities could now fit into the framework of your officially accepted life.2 Such musings, with desire -- backed up by common sense -- can bring about intersection points in probabilities that cause a fresh realignment of the deep elements of the psyche. In such ways probable events can be attracted to your current living structure.
quotes.translation: 现在：拿起另一张你在不同年龄的照片，不同于你选的第一张。简单地问自己："我看的是同一个人吗？"这第二张照片有多熟悉或多陌生？它与你今晚选的第一张照片有何不同？在你的脑海中，有哪些相似之处将这两张照片联系起来？每张照片拍摄时你经历了什么？在一张照片中你曾考虑追随的方向，在另一张中却没有实现？那些方向其实已被追寻。如果它们没有被你所认知的自我追寻，那么它们就是被你所谓的"可能的自我"所追寻。在你的脑海中，追随那个自我本会采取的方向，如同你思考这些事件一样。如果你发现一条你希望自己曾追随却未追随的发展路线，那么请深入思考这些活动现在如何能融入你官方接受的生活框架。这样的沉思，加上欲望——以及常识的支持——可以在概率中创造交叉点，导致心灵深层元素的全新重组。通过这种方式，可能的事件可以被吸引到你当前的生活结构中。

source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: Your experience of yourself marks the seeming boundaries of yourself. In a manner of speaking, I am one personality and one program or station. Ruburt is another. We have learned to be aware of each other* to communicate between stations, to affect each other's programs and to change each other's worlds. I do not speak alone to Ruburt and Joseph, for example, but my words go out to the world that you know. Still within your framework, Ruburt tunes in to another station, translates it and broadcasts the information. To do this, however, he has to alter his own consciousness, withdraw momentarily from the official station to bring in this one. That means tuning in to other portions of the psyche, as well as another kind of reality. The final translation of my material has to come through his organism, however, or it would be meaningless to you.
quotes.translation: 你对自我的体验标记了自我的表面边界。打个比方说，我是一个人格、一个节目或一个电台。鲁柏是另一个。我们学会了彼此觉察，在电台之间沟通，影响彼此的节目，改变彼此的世界。例如，我并非只对鲁柏和约瑟夫说话，而是我的话语传向你所知的世界。然而，在你的框架内，鲁柏调谐到另一个电台，翻译它，并广播信息。但要做到这一点，他必须改变自己的意识，暂时从官方电台抽离，以接入这个电台。这意味着要调谐到心灵的其他部分，以及另一种现实。然而，我材料的最终翻译必须通过他的有机体完成，否则对你而言将毫无意义。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
